1- Purpose 2- Scope 3 - References Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List
9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms 1- Purpose: This SOP aims to define the process for collecting and verifying data for new clients and demographic   updates   for  existing  groups, ensuring accurate and  timely   information for credentialing: It outlines steps for data   collection,  verification,  follow-ups, and final uploading: 2-Scope: This SOP outlines the standardized process for collecting, verifying, and uploading critical data for new clients and   demographic   updates within the credentialing   department: Its purpose is to ensure that   anyone who reads this   SOP  understands how information is gathered, verified, and utilized to maintain   compliance, accuracy, and   integrity in the credentialing process_ This procedure applies to both new client onboardings and updates to existing group data, detailing the steps for data collection__ document verification; communication with the Account Manager O provider, and final data uploading to relevant platforms_
3-References: The International Standard ISO 9001.2015.

CS Uploads the group documents in the group subfile and names them Document X Group Name: CS  uploads the provider documents in the   provider   subfile and names them Document Y Provider' s name_ Once all data and requirements are verified and agreed upon: CS informs SCS that the client is fully onboarded SCS updates SDD The credentialing process starts based on the provider' $ preferences.

9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms
1 Purpose:
This SOP defines the standardized process for collecting, verifying, and uploading critical data for new Physical Therapy providers joining PT of The City (PTOC) It aims to ensure accurate and timely information for credentialing, system setup, and compliance, outlining steps for data collection, verification, follow-ups, and final uploading to relevant platforms
2- Scope: This   SOP applies to all Credentialing Staff and  cross-departmental teams (e.g;, Staffing; Executive Management)  involved in the onboarding of new PTOC providers for clinic assignments or home therapy services.  It covers the process from receipt of the hiring clearance through to the providers data being fully integrated into all required systems.
3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- Internal "Credentialing Manual" Sheet:

4- Terms Terminology: 4.1-Abbreviation:
4.1.1- CAQH: Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare 4.1.2- CMS Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services 4.13-CS Credentialing Specialist 4.1.44- MEP Medicaid Enrolled Provider 4.1.5- MMIS#: Medicaid Management Information System Number 4.1.6- NPI National Provider Identifier 4.1.7- Pecos Provider Enrollment, Chain, and Ownership System 4.1.8- PTOC : Physical Therapy of The City 4.1.9- SSN Social Security Number 4.1.10- USPS: United States Postal Services 4.1.11-WC Worker' s Compensation 4.2- Terms: 4.2.1- CAQH: is platform where healthcare   providers can enter and maintain their professional and practice information; such as work experience, practice addresses, and education that helps ensure that all their credentials are up to date and health plans can access this information for the credentialing process 4.2.2- ClickUp: platform used by the Credentialing department to help manage tasks, projects, and workflows. 4.3- Credentialing Plan: A sheet where all PTOC locations are added in sub-sheets is used to specify which rendering provider is at which servicing location: 4.2.4- Data Collection: is the process of collecting and verifying the data provided by the provider in the onboarding stage. 4.2.5- Demographic Update: a process of updating the provider and/or group information with insurance companies, adding a new service location under the group, Or adding location to a provider under the group_ 4.2.6- Google Drive File: A file created by the CS for each provider under PTOC is used by the CS to store the provider documents _

4.2.7- Hiring Clearance email: Hiring Clearance is an email received from the staffing department confirming that a new provider has completed the oboarding process and is cleared to begin working: It includes relevant details about the providers status with all required data and documents to begin and readiness to start providing services_ 4.2.8- Home Therapy: PT Providers   under PT of The City Group offer personalized physical therapy services to patients  in their homes. These  providers  focus on delivering convenient; in-home care tailored to individual patient needs_ 4.2.9- Location list: list on the PTOC space on ClickUp that has the tasks of provider/group to document and track updates and information of providers/groups. 4.2.10- Login Sheet: It's a Google sheet for PTOC that includes all the information used by any related party working on PTOC to help the daily process 4.2.11- Malpractice: It' $ shield for doctors. If a patient sues them for mistake, the insurance helps cover the costs. This gives doctors peace of mind to focus on their patients_ 4.2.12- MEP Lookup: lookup is used to get the MMIS# of enrolled providers_ 4.2.13- NPI: It' s unique identifying number for any healthcare provider O group and organization used in the U.S. 4.2.14- NPPES lookup: lookup used to verify the provider and/or group NPI 4.2.15- PTOC Agenda: a google sheet for internal departmental use that tracks provider information, credentialing process of all providers, and contains email templates and location details. 4.2.16- Registration License: It' $ license provided by the state that permits the provider to practice in this state_ 4.2.17- Rendering provider: the provider that is providing the services. 4.2.18- Servicing location: Clinic's location where the healthcare services are provided to the patients. 4.2.19- SSN Card: is a physical card issued by the Social Security Administration (SSA) in the U.S: that contains unique   nine-digit   number used for identification, employment; and tax purposes_

4.2.20- State License: It' $ certification to ensure that all practicing physicians have appropriate education and training and that they abide by professional standards while serving their patients_ 4.2.21- Therabill: is a billing system designed for healthcare providers, particularly in the behavioral health   field. It helps manage   patient billing; insurance   claims, and payment tracking, making the billing process easier and more efficient for practices. 4.2.22- USPS lookup: lookup that helps you find the postal code (Zip Code) for any address in the United States. It allows you to enter an address, and it will provide the correct Zip Code for that location and correct it: 4.2.23- Worker'  $ Compensation#: is unique identifier assigned to healthcare providers who treat patients under workers' compensation claims_ 5-Responsibility for _Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Senior Credentialing Responsible for the overall oversight  of the  procedure, Specialist (SCS) assigning tasks, and ensuring compliance. Credentialing Responsible for executing the data collection, verification; Specialist (CS) follow-up, and uploading steps as outlined in this procedure. Responsible for   triggering the process by   sending the PTOC HR Team complete "Hiring Clearance" task to the Credentialing team 6_Procedures: 6.1- Data Collection and Verification for New PTOC Provider: 6.1.1- Announcement: The HR team announces the new provider by submitting the "Hiring Clearance" task to the Credentialing team via Clickup (PSICRE-F-06). The task must include: Provider's full name and assigned location (clinic or home therapy). All required documents: Updated resume, active malpractice insurance (PLI), state license, registration license, SSN, date of birth; and professional photo (for ZocDoc). The SCS assigns the responsible CS to the task:

CS uploads all documents as PDFs with standardized filenames: [Document Type] [Provider Name] on the drive folder: CS creates folder on Clickup on the 'PT of the City Locations space named [Provider First Name Last Name]: CS collects the information from the forms and reflects it 0 the Clickup folder (PSICRE-F-04): 6.1.3- Data Verification: CS takes the collected data and verifies them through the following methods: Data Verification Method Provider's Name From SSN, driver' s license, or state license Date of Birth Passport or  driver license; ask HR or provider directly if (DOB) missing NPPES   lookup  (confirm specialty,  provider name, linked NPI license number, and NPI status)  (Appendix 4) SSN SSN Card Confirm policy document includes provider $ name, coverage Malpractice dates, and active status Validate license number and name via state board (Appendix State License 2) Registration Confirm temporary/provisional license details License License Number State licensing board lookup (active status and name match) Updated Resume Verify work history, education; and certifications are current Individual MMIS# MEP Lookup tool (Appendix 3) Worker' s Validate through state Workers Compensation lookup Compensation# (Appendix 5)

If deficiencies are identified, initiate follow-up with the HR team to obtain the missing documents_ In cases where the resume is missing some information, CS can reach out to the provider directly. 6.1.4- Verified Data Uploading: CS uploads the verified data on the *PTOC Agenda sheet on the *PTOC Providers sub sheet. (Appendix 5) 6.2- Credentialed PTs for Home Therapy Only: 6.2.1- Announcement & Initiation: Receipt of the "Hiring Clearance" email from the Staffing Department; which must specify the provider's designation as 'Home Therapy" The assigned CS confirms receipt and reviews the email 6.2.2- Data Collection & Folder Setup: The CS creates a dedicated provider folder in the PTOC Google Drive. A subfolder is created named [Provider First Name Last Name Home Therapy]: All provided documents are uploaded as PDFs with standardized filenames: [Document Type] [Provider First Name Last Name]_ 6.2.3- Data Verification: The CS verifies all  individual  provider: The verification criteria are identical to Section 6.1.3 for clinic providers The   "Servicing  Location" is   administratively set as   "Home Visits   Therapy PTOC" with the address of "Mid-town PTOC HeadQuarter" _ This is a system requirement and does not require physical address verification 6.2.4- Data Uploading System Integration (Home Therapy Specific): The provider is added to ClickUp under the "Home Visits Therapy PTOC" list. The Servicing Location must be set to: "Home Visits Therapy PTOC (Mid-town PTOC HeadQuarter)" All other verified data (Name, DOB, SSN, NPI, etc. is entered as standard. The PTOC Agenda Sheet is updated with the new provider'$ information: The "Servicing Location" column must clearly state "Home Therapy"

No action is required. 6.2.5- Completion: Once all data and requirements are verified and uploaded to the specified systems, the home therapy provider is considered fully oboarded, and the credentialing process begins. 6.3- Adding a New Servicing Location/Clinic: 6.3.1- Announcement: Obtain a formal email from the Executive/Technical Manager confirming the new service location, including: Full address_ Operational start date_ Name of the service location/clinic. USPS Address Validation: Verify the address using the USPS Address Validation Tool. Invalid Address: Collaborate with the Credentialing Lead, Executive, and Technical Manager that the address is not correct and take the proper action Valid Address: Proceed to database updates. 6.3.2- Data Collection & Verification: CS confirms receipt of the announcement email. Servicing Address: Verified using the USPS Zip Code Lookup tool. Invalid Address: CS escalates to the Credentialing Lead, who collaborates with the Executive and Technical Manager to resolve. Valid Address: Proceed to system updates 6.3.3- System Integration: PT of the City Login Sheet: CS adds the new address to the "Service Locations" section_ ClickUp: CS creates folder labeled with the location name, generates a specific list, and assigns tasks to the responsible CS. Therabill: CS adds the new service location to Therabill once the operational date is confirmed.

11- Appendices and Forms 1- Purpose: The objective of this procedure is to establish a clear and standardized method for processing demographic updates and adding new providers to groups This   process   aims to ensure accuracy, timely completion; and consistent tracking of changes, maintaining compliance with the credentialing requirements. 2- Scope: This procedure outlines the standardized process for collecting, verifying, and processing demographic updates for existing providers O adding new providers to group within the credentialing department: Its purpose is to ensure that all team members follow consistent approach for   gathering,  verifying, and  submitting provider  demographic  information to maintain accuracy, compliance, and integrity in the credentialing process 3-References:

3.2- Credentialing Manual

AM creates task on the  Onboarding/Expansion   list on the    Operations Hub- External Clients space 0n ClickUp with the new information 6.2.2- The data is collected and verified_ check the Data Collection  and verification procedure_ 6.2.3- Proceed with demographic updates: CS proceeds with the demographic update based 0n the provider'$ network status (either INN or OON): In-network enrollment: If website submission: CS checks credentialing manual for steps and link: Fills the form on the website and reviews the inserted data and submits the form Documents the action taken on the location list on the CEA space on ClickUp using a template (code: PSICRE-F-07). Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to Demo update in process and sets a due date_ If Email Submission: CS checks   credentialing manual for  steps; formls and insurance email address. Fills the form and reviews the inserted data_ Writes the email using a template and attaches the filled form/s along with the required documents. Sends the enrollment email with the subject 'Demographic Update Request {Group 's Name} Documents the action taken on the location list' on the CEA space on ClickUp using a template (code: PSICRE-F-07). Assigns themselves to the task, changes the task status to Demo update in process and sets a due date.

If form/s are returned, CS contacts the provider to mail them again. If delivered, CS contacts the insurance again and investigates_ If no tracking no.was provided: CS allows more time, then calls the insurance again to check If still not  received, CS asks the provider to mail the documents again. CONTENTS: 1- Purpose 2- Scope 3 - References 4- Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms LPurpose: This   procedure aims to establish standardized and efficient process for   credentialing specialists to set up Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) for healthcare providers, ensuring accuracy; compliance, and timeliness in the processing of the enrollments 2- Scope: 2.1-This procedure applies to all credentialing specialists responsible for EDI and ERA setup: It outlines the entire process; including: Receiving the setup requests Submitting enrollments Monitoring enrollment status Ensuring accurate Completion of the Enrollments 2.2- By following this procedure, credentialing specialists can maintain consistency, reduce errors, and improve the efficiency of the EDI & ERA Setup. 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- Credentialing Training Manual 3.3- Procedures for Data Collection 3.4- Medicare (NGS) and Medicaid (ETIN) enrollment regulations

4- Terms Terminology: 4.1-Abbreviation: 4.1.1-AM Account Manager 4.1.2- B&AD: Billing & Authorization Department 4.13-CS Credentialing Specialist 4.1.4- EDI Electronic Data Interchange 4.1.5- EEL EDI Enrollments List (tracked in ClickUp) 4.1.6- (E&PS): EDIs and Portals Space (ClickUp workspace) 4.1.7- EOB explanation of benefits 4.1.8- ERA Electronic Remittance Advice 4.1.9- ETIN Electronic Transmitter Identification Number 4.1.10- NGS National Government Services 4.1.11- PID Packet ID 4.1.12- SDD Service Delivery Department 4.2-Terms and Definitions: 4.2.1- Electronic Data Interchange (EDI): It is a process that allows the submission of electronic claims to insurance companies. 4.2.2- Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA): It is the electronic version of paper EOBs and provides details about claim payments 4.2.3- ERA &EDI enrollment: the process of linking the billing software to its integrated clearinghouse in order to submit claims and receive the electronic responses 4.2.4- Electronic Transmitter Identification Number (ETIN): It's a four-digit billing code that allows providers to electronically submit claims and check eligibility for Medicaid in New York: 4.2.5- Packet ID (PID): A unique identification number assigned to a packet of enrollment information submitted through the NGS Medicare Enrollment Portal 4.2.6- National Government Services (NGS): Medicare Administrative Contractor (MAC) that processes Medicare claims and provides other services to healthcare providers. 4.2.7- ClickUp: Project management platform used for task submission, status updates, and logging issues.

6_ Procedures: 6.1- ERA Setup Procedure: 6.1.1- Receive the ERA setup request: The credentialing specialist (CS) receives request from the (B&AD) o (SDD) after the provider' $ insurance enrollment is completed. In this task the CS should be provided by the Payer Name and Payer ID from SDD or B&A department (the department who creates the task) and the task is reflected in the (EEL) in the EDIs and Portals Space on ClickUp. CS assigns a due date for this task to ensure it is not forgotten. 6.1.2- Identifying the Billing System: The credentialing specialist (CS) confirms the provider's preferred billing system for ERA submission with the account manager (AM) The process varies based on the type of billing system: If the provider requires specific billing system (one account for one group, the Account Manager (AM) is responsible for setting up the system for that provider: If the provider will be using an existing, common billing system (used by multiple groups): The Account Manager (AM) updates the billing system by adding the provider's Tax ID_ Credentialing Specialist (CS) updates the EDI Enrollments folder name of the group to accurately reflect the provider' $ designated billing - system_ 6.13- ERA Enrollment Submission: CS retrieves login credentials for the billing system from the credentials sub-sheet in the manual o directly from the login sheet (Code: PSICRE-F-0S) for each group, depending on the billing system used. CS accesses the ERA Enrollment section within the billing system: Depending on the system, this process may vary: In Billing systems, CS may need to enter the group name and then search using: Payer ID or Payer Name and navigate to the ERA enrollment Section.

In other systems, no need to enter the group name as the account is already linked to the Tax ID of the group. CS follows the specific ERA enrollment steps   for the billing system  used: And enters the required information of the group that was collected in the Data Collection Step (referencing the procedure of Data Collection as a guide) CS submits the ERA, and the method of submission depends on the billing system: Direct Enrollment (If Applicable): Some billing systems allow for direct ERA enrollment within the system itself, and CS follows those specific steps. Via Email: Some Billing systems may require an ERA enrollment submission via email.
CS checks if the email address for the enrollment submission is listed in the Manual under EDIERA/EFT section_ If the email is not updated, CS updates the manual with the correct address provided in the form obtained from the billing - system: CS composes an email with the following details: Subject: ERA Enrollment Request Body includes: Provider Information Attaches ERA enrollment Form (if applicable) Request for confirmation of receipt 6.1.4- Update of ClickUp: CS updates the task in the EDI EEL 0 ClickUp to track all interactions with the clearinghouse /Practice Mate and ensures complete record of the process The updates should include: Status of the task updated to 'In Process Date of the ERA setup Submission Accurate comment 0 the update and the Submission Method (Via the Billing system Portal or email) Submission Ticket number (If applicable) Email address and Email subject (For Emails)

6.1.5- ERA Enrollment Follow-Up Process: If ERA enrollment is submitted through the Billing System: CS logs into the billing system to check the enrollment status The status could be: Pending: The enrollment is still being processed. Payer Received: The payer has received the enrollment; If there is any delay O the status is not progressing; the CS will submit support ticket to investigate if additional actions are needed from the credentialing team: If ERA Enrollment is submitted through Email: Follow up by sending an email to the designated email address to inquire about the enrollment status_ Document the Follow-Up process: CS documents all follow-up dates (when the follow-up occurred): CS records the method of follow-up whether it was through the billing system or email). CS updates the Task in ClickUp with the next action to be taken; ensuring that the next steps are clear for further follow-up. 6.1.6- Completion of ERA Setup: The CS confirms that the EDI ERA setup is complete using one of the following methods: Billing System Status Update: The CS checks the billing system, where the status will change to "Payer Approved", confirming that the setup is complete. Provider Notification: The insurance   provider notifies the provider about the completed setup: The provider then forwards this confirmation to the credentialing department: Direct Notification: The insurance provider directly informs the credentialing department that the setup is complete: Once the enrollment is confirmed as completed, the CS takes the following actions: The CS records the effective date of the ERA enrollment in the EDI Enrollments List on ClickUp marks the task as Complete in ClickUp to indicate the process is finalized. Updates the EFT & ERA sub-sheet within the login sheet (code: PSICRE-F-13) of the group to reflect the completed enrollment:

6.2- EDI Setup: CS  submits the EDI enrollment aS part of the provider $ insurance enrollment process_ 6.2.1- Medicaid EDI Enrollment Process: CS fills out the ETIN enrollment form (Code: PSICRE-F-11) with the grouplprovider information for the Medicaid enrollment. Sends   the   completed form to the   provider via email for their   signature and notarization. Note: Electronic notarization is not accepted. Instructs the provider to submit the notarized document via mail. The provider also shares the tracking number with CS for the follow-up process_ Updates the tracking number in the ETIN list within the assigned group folder for tracking purposes_ Once the enrollment with Medicaid is completed, the CS ensures that the ETIN is activated and that the four-digit ETIN is provided and recorded in the ETIN list: CS updates the effective date and expiration date of the ETIN in the ETIN list: Before the ETIN expiration date, the CS sends an ETIN recertification form (Code PSICRE-F-12) to the provider and follows the same submission instructions for this process: AlL the steps should be documented on ClickUp with the date of each process and the update. 6.2.2- Medicare EDI Enrollment process: After the provider enrollment is completed, CS logs into the Billing System to begin the EDI enrollment process_ The CS accesses the NGS Medicare Link to initiate the enrollment process for the provider The CS enters the group information and clearinghouse (or Practice Mate) details into the system. After entering the required information and Submission of the EDI Enrollment, the system generates Packet ID (PID), for the tracking of the enrollment: CS then updates EDI Task in EDI enrollment list specific to the group under the EDI and Portal Space in ClickUp, with the date of the submission and Packet ID for reference_

CS updates ClickUp with the following: The date of the follow-up (when the CS checked the status). The status of the enrollment (e.g , "Pending" Approved" , "Completed" , etc.). Once the Medicare   enrollment process is fully completed, the CS updates the ClickUp task: CS Marks the task as "Completed" to indicate that the process is finished. CS updates the task with the effective date of the EDI enrollment: 6.2.3- Commercial Payers EDI enrollment is typically automatic and does not require additional action 7-Revision Amendment Log: Revision # Date Changes 01.01.2026 Document Creation

Billing & Authorization Responsible for initiating the EFT sctup process by Dept (B&AD) Service submitting a task in ClickUp after insurance enrollment is Delivery Dept (SDD) complete: 6_ Procedures: 6.1- Task Initiation and Data Preparation 6.1.1- Announcement: Trigger: The credentialing specialist (CS) receives task in the "EDI Enrollments List" within the "EDIs and Portals" space on ClickUp from B&AD or SDD: Action: The CS sets a due date for the task to ensure timely completion. 6.1.2- Data Collection & Verification: The CS collects and verifies the required   information,   referencing the "Data Collection" procedure where applicable: Group/Individual Info: Tax ID, NPI (verified via NPPES lookup Appendix 4), Group Name, Provider Name Service & Billing Address: Verified via USPS lookup (Appendix 6). Handling Missing Data (IBVW): If any information of the group, billing address, voided check; or W9 is missing, the CS requests it from the AM and follows up until obtained. System Update: The CS updates ClickUp and the designated Google Drive with all collected data and documents_ 6.2- EFT Enrollment Submission 6.2.1- Determine Submission Method: The CS contacts the insurance provider to determine the EFT setup method: Direct Enrollment or via a clearinghouse. 6.2.2- Execute Enrollment: The CS follows the method-specific steps below, referencing the internal credentialing manual and the "Portal Creation" procedure. Submission Method CS Action 1. Access the portal/website following the steps in the manual. Direct 2. Navigate to EFT section. (Portal/Website) 3. Complete the form with IBVW data. 4. Submit and note the ticket/reference number: 1. Send email to designated address in the manual. 2_ Include provider info (Group Name, Tax ID, NPI; Address) and Direct (Email) list attachments_ 3. Request confirmation: Log in to clearinghouse portal use credentials from Login Sheet of the client). Via Clearinghouse 2. Navigate to EFT section. 3. Complete the form with IBVW data. 4. Submit: Ensure status reaches "Payer Received" . Note: EFT enrollments require payer-specific digital form submissions These forms are external and controlled by the respective payer, not by Practice Solution. Issue Resolution: If any issues arise during submission, the CS contacts the respective technical support (portal, website, or clearinghouse) to resolve them before rC - submitting:

6.2.3- Task Documentation: The CS immediately updates the ClickUp task with: Status: "In Process" Submission Date, Method, and Ticket/Reference Number Representative name (if called) or Email Subject 6.3- Follow-up and Completion 6.3.1- Follow-up Process: The CS follows up based on the submission method to confirm receipt and check status. Direct/Portal/Email: Follow up via phone Or email, referencing the ticket ID. Clearinghouse: Log in to check status; contact clearinghouse support if delayed; confirm with insurance that they received the enrollment_ Documentation: The CS documents all  follow-up actions in the ClickUp task (date, method, contact, status, next steps). 6.3.2- Handling missing information identified during follow-up: If the insurance requests additional data, the CS obtains it, submits it via the requested method (email/fax), and follows up to confirm receipt. 6.3.3- Completion: Confirmation: The CS confirms EFT setup is complete via one of: direct confirmation from insurance, "Payer Approved" status in a portal, O official notification_ Final  Documentation: The CS obtains the effective date, marks the ClickUp task as "Complete' and updates the EFT & ERA sub-sheet in the group's Login Sheet (code: PSICRE-F-13). 6.4- Handling Post-Setup Issues 6.4.1- Issue Intake: Trigger: B&AD or SDD submits a task in the "Demands and Requests or "Billing Forms  space on ClickUp regarding an EFT issue. Action: The CS investigates the issue by contacting the insurance provider: 6.4.2- Resolution: The CS takes corrective action, which may include requesting additional info from the provider O coordinating with the insurance/clearinghouse. It aims to maintain compliance, accuracy, and ensure that all enrollments are processed in a timely and organized manner_ 2- Scope: This procedure is for Credentialing Specialists (CS) and details the steps to submit enrollment applications  for healthcare providers and groups into insurance  networks (both INN and OON): This procedure includes determining the enrollment method (website, email, fax, Or mail), submitting forms and following up on submissions, handling rejections O missing forms, and updating platforms like ClickUp and the AIL subsheet: The scope covers  all stages from initial data collection to enrollment confirmation and final updates 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- Data Collection and Verification Procedure 3.3- Credentialing Manual 3.4- Portal Creation and Management Procedure

4.2-Terms and Definition: 4.2.1- Accepted Insurance List (AIL): A list that contains all the insurance plans the provider is enrolled with; including network status and effective dates_ 4.2.2 - AIL Subsheet: A subsheet in the Login Sheet used to track the provider's insurance enrollments, indicating network status (INN o OON) and effective dates for each insurance_ 4.2.3- Credentialing External Accounts (CEA): A dedicated space 0 ClickUp used by the Credentialing Department to manage all external client files and tasks 4.2.4 Credentialing Manual: A comprehensive guide used by the Credentialing Department for enrollment processes, demographic updates, EFT/ERA/EDI setup, and insurance-specific contact information_ 4.2.5- Credentialing Plan: A subsheet in the Login Sheet used to specify which rendering provider should be billed at which servicing location (code: PSICRE-F-14).

4.2.6- Effective Date: The date when insurance enrollment   becomes active and the provider can begin seeing patients under that insurance plan. 4.2.7- Escalation: The   process of seeking assistance from senior  staff  or alternative contacts  when standard follow-up methods fail to resolve an issue after multiple attempts (typically 4 follow-ups). 4.2.8- INN Email Thread: A monthly email thread created by the Credentialing Manager to notify the Billing Authorization Department and Service Delivery Department about new enrollments and demographic updates. 4.2.9- Location List: task list within the CEA space on   ClickUp that   tracks and documents all updates and information related to providers and groups 4.2.10- Login   Sheet: A centralized spreadsheet for each client containing all provider information; insurance details, credentials, and access information used by the credentialing team and related parties 4.2.11- Network Preference: The provider's choice to participate as either In-Network (INN) o Out-of-Network (OON) with specific insurance payers_ 4.2.12- Network Status: Indicates   whether provider is In-Network (INN) o Out-of- Network (OON) with specific insurance plan. 4.2.13- Portal: An online system used by insurance companies for electronic enrollment submissions, tracking, and management: 4.2.14- Provider: healthcare   professional or entity  being   enrolled with insurance networks to provide medical services. 4.2.15- Rendering Provider: The individual  healthcare provider who directly delivers services to patients 4.2.16- Service Location: The physical address where healthcare services are provided to patients_ 4.2.17- USPS Tracking Lookup: An online tool used to track  mailed packages and confirm delivery status. CS checks manual for the method of communication with the insurance_ CS calls Or emails provider relations or services and provides either the reference number or the date they received the application: CS documents the action taken on the task on location list' on the CEA space following the template (code: PSICRE-F-07) If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date. If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow- ups; seeks alternate PRIS contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact then follow up till the enrollment is completed. If email submission:
CS sends a follow-up email on the same request that was sent: CS documents the action taken on the task on location list' on the CEA space following the template If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date. If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow- ups; seeks alternate PRIS contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact then follow up till the enrollment is completed. If fax submission: CS calls PRIS and provides the date of submission. CS documents the action taken on the task on location list" on the CEA space following the template If there are updates the CS changes the task status and the due date_ If there are no updates the CS changes the task due date, escalates after 4 follow- ups; seeks alternate PR/S contacts for updates and asks for an updated contact then follow up till the enrollment is completed.

Additionally, it supports credentialing processes, including the demo update for certain insurance providers 2- Scope: This procedure  outlines the procedures for the creation and ongoing support of provider portals within the   Credentialing   Department: It encompasses   the full   lifecycle of these portals,  from   initial   setup and registration to continuous maintenance, ensuring efficient functionality for billing and credentialing purposes 3_References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- Internal "Credentialing Manual" Sheet 3.3- Procedures: Data Collection and Verification 3.4- Procedure: Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) Setup

In order words, it encompasses everything that can be seen and touched, whether internal or external: 4.3- Software: Software is collection of programs   commands, and instructions that control how computers and all electronic operate. In other words, it's the intangible part of the device that directs the hardware to perform its tasks_
5-Responsibility for_Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes_ CEO Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes _ Using assigned devices according to IT policies. Maintaining device and account security: All employee Reporting technical issues promptly. Returning all devices and access accounts during offboarding: Using company emails and systems strictly for work purposes: All Managers Ensuring employees follow IT policies and device usage rules_ Supervisors Approving device requests and access permissions Preparing, configuring; and issuing devices to employees. Troubleshooting and resolving technical issues. IT Department Maintaining device records and updating inventory_ Ensuring proper setup of accounts, emails, and system access_ Collecting and checking devices during_ offboarding: 6- Procedures: 6.1-IT Onboarding: 6.1.1-Once the hiring process is completed and the new hire'$ start date is confirmed, HR sends an email to the IT Department informing them of the new employee and requesting the preparation of the assigned laptop. 6.1.2- Upon receiving HR's email notification, the IT Department prepares the laptop, completes the required details in receipt acknowledgment form (code: PSHIT-F-01) and records the asset information before handing it over to the new hire.

6.2- IT Support: 6.2.1- The employee reports the laptop issue to their direct manager immediately after the problem occurs_ 6.2.2- Upon  manager approval, the employee proceeds to the IT department submits support ticket via Email or via Voice describing the problem in support request form code (PS/OpEx-F-01) (type of issue, when it occurred, any relevant notes). 6.2.3- The IT technician performs an initial assessment of the device to identify the cause and type of issue (software or hardware related) 6.2.4- After evaluation, the technician informs the employee of the estimated time required for repair on the ticket 6.2.5- Minor issues are repaired internally by the IT technician within the estimated time_ If issues include device damage or require hardware replacement, the IT technician coordinates with management to investigate the possibility of repair O replacement: 6.2.6- Once the issue is  resolved or an action  plan is determined, the IT technician completes the fault report form, (code: PS/IT-F-02), which includes: Employee name and device information Description of the issue Actions taken Date and time of resolution Technician 's signature This report is retained for at least three years. 6.2.7- Once the issue is resolved, the IT technician closes the ticket and retaining it for at least 3 years_ 6.2.8- The IT technician   produces monthly   report (code: PS/IT-F-04) summarizing incidents and repair times for performance analysis

It applies to the use or management of company assets_ 3-References: The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Software License: legal agreement between the software creator and the end user, granting rights to use and distribute the software under specific terms. It outlines user responsibilities, limits claims against the owner; and may restrict how the software can legally be used. 4.2- Item Tag: unique identification code assigned to each controlled item 4.3- Quarantine Area: designated area for devices that are unfit for use Or under inspection.

6- Procedures: 6.1- Receiving Items: 6.1.1- IT specialist receives items against approved purchase orders_ 6.1.2- IT specialist verifies quantity, specifications, serial numbers, warrantee documents, and item condition before signing off the receipt IT specialist refuses receipt if item quality or quantity do not match the purchase order and informs procurement: 6.1.3- Record the receiving item in receiving voucher form (code: PS/IT-F-0S) 6.1.4- Record the receiving item in IT receiving register (code: PS/IT-F-06), and the item register (code: PS/IT-F-07). 6.1.5- In case of item return, the IT specialist records the reason of return and any addition details in return form (code: PSMIT-F-09). 6.2- Storage: 6.2.1- Equipment is stored in secured, access-controlled areas. 6.2.2- Items are classified using item tags, item tags serve to uniquely identify items throughout their life cycle within the company. 6.3- Software License Control: 6.3.1- IT specialists maintain the software license register, monitor validity and usage. IT specialists never allow unlicensed or unapproved software installation:

7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement
11- Appendices and Forms 1- Purpose: To define the purchasing procedures and the criteria for evaluating service providers, as well as conducting periodic evaluations to ensure fulfillment of supply commitments. To define and describe the purchasing process to verify that purchased materials conform to the required specifications 2- Scope: This   procedure   applies to all  purchasing activities and   supplier   evaluations   related to products, services, software, and IT-related supplies. 3-References: The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Requirements: general  term  referring to all materials and services required by the company for production and operational activities. 4.2- Materials: general term referring to all tangible items required by the company, such as supplies, spare parts, and similar items_

4.3- Services:
general term referring to all intangible items required to ensure the continuity of the company operations and production, such as maintenance, training, and consultancy services. 4.4- Requirements Planning: The process of identifying requirements and   determining the methods and means of procuring them in accordance with a defined time plan_ 4.5- Purchase Requisition: form prepared by the requesting department to specify the quantity, specifications of the item to be purchased, the required delivery dates, and the delivery location 4.6- Request for Quotation (RFQ): form   prepared by the Head   of the Local Purchasing Department to obtain price quotations from different suppliers for the same item. 4.7- Purchase Order: form prepared by the Purchasing Manager to execute the purchasing process with the selected supplier: 5-Responsibility for_Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes_ CEO Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes. IT Manager Approves specifications, allocation, and disposal. 6- Procedures:
6.1- Identification of Purchasing Needs: 6.1.1- The requesting department shall inform the information technology department of its purchasing requirements.

6.1.2- A purchase requisition form (code: PS/IT-F-12) shall be prepared and submitted to the purchasing officer: The form shall include the following details: All relevant purchasing data Item name, reason for request, unit of measure, required quantity, and current stock balance Required delivery period Requesting department and justification Required quantity and delivery timeline 6.1.3- The Purchase Requisition shall be approved by the requesting department either by signature or via official email. 6.2- Purchase Requisition Approval Process: 6.2.1- The purchasing officer shall collect all purchase requisitions and submit them to the company executive manager for approval: 6.3- Purchasing Execution Process: 6.3.1- Upon approval of the purchase requisition, the head of the IT department shall perform the following: Select the appropriate supplier from the approved supplier list (code: PS/IT-F-13). Issue purchase   order (code: PSIIT-F-14), approved by the finance manager, including the following information: Item name and item code as stated in the Purchase Requisition Quantity to be supplied and delivery schedule (where applicable) Purchase Requisition number and supplier quotation reference (if available) Delivery location (if applicable) 6.3.2- If the item is available from more than one approved supplier; price quotations shall be obtained using the request for quotation (RFQ) Form (code: PSIT-F-15), and comparison shall be conducted to select the most competitive offer: 6.3.3- If the required item is not available from approved suppliers list (code: PS/IT-F-13), the head of the local purchasing department shall request quotations  from non- approved suppliers based on the value of the purchase.

6.3.4- Additional evaluation actions shall be taken to select and approve the supplier prior to completing the purchase and document the evaluation in evaluation suppliers form (code: PSIT-F-19). 6.3.5- Any special terms and conditions related to the purchase order (code: PS/IT-F-14) shall be printed on the reverse side and shall be considered an integral part of the purchase order. 6.3.6- The purchasing officer shall distribute copies of the approved purchase order as follows: Original copy to the supplier for execution One copy retained by the purchasing department (electronic or hard copy) 6.3.7- The purchasing officer shall follow up O purchase requisitions (code: PS/IT-F-12) and purchase orders (code: PS/IT-F-14) with suppliers until delivery and inspection are completed, based on the purchase follow-up register (code: PS/IT-F-16). 6.3.8- Upon delivery of purchased items, the storekeeper shall acknowledge receipt by signing the supplier'$ delivery note or copy of the purchase invoice, indicating that the items are received subject to inspection. 6.4- Price Comparison and Supplier Selection Process: 6.4.1- The purchasing officer shall compare supplier quotations received in response to RFQs (code: PSMIT-F-17) when the same item with identical  specifications and quality level is available from more than one approved supplier: 6.4.2- The purchasing officer shall ensure that all quotations are complete, clear; and properly documented. 6.4.3- After evaluating the quotations, the purchasing officer shall select the best offer and issue the purchase order (code: PS/IT-F-14) to the selected supplier: 6.5- Procurement of Services: 6.5.1- The purchasing officer  shall be responsible for contacting and dealing with all service providers, including but not limited to: Maintenance service providers_ Training and consultancy companies_ Any other service providers required by the company_

1- Purpose 2- Scope 3- References 4. Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement
11- Appendices and Forms 1 Purpose: The objective of this procedure is to establish systematic and standardized process  for auditing medical notes to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with organizational, regulatory, and quality standards This procedure provides guidance for reviewing all patient encounters, ensuring that every appointment has corresponding documented note in the system: It also outlines the management of complex, incomplete, or denied notes to guarantee proper resolution and maintain documentation integrity. The organization aims to enhance the reliability of medical records, support continuity of patient care, reduce the risk of errors Or omissions, and ensure that all documentation meets clinical, legal, and operational requirements. Additionally, the process promotes accountability among healthcare providers, facilitates   transparent   reporting, and supports continuous  quality improvement initiatives within the medical auditing framework: 2-Scope: 2.1- Applies to the Medical Auditing Team and all staff involved in medical record review and documentation_ 2.2- Covers all medical notes done by clients medical staff to identify, confirm, and resolve missing, inconsistent; or incomplete medical notes_

3-References: 3.1- The Internation Standard ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Systems 3.2- HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) 3.3- CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) Guidelines Documentation, billing; and compliance requirements 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- EMR system: It is an organized collection of patient'$ information that is stored electronically and can be accessed and updated by authorized healthcare providers_ 4.2- Medical Audit: systematic review of patient records, billing documentation, and coding accuracy to ensure compliance and accuracy 4.3- Patient Medical Record: A version of a patient's medical history and healthcare data, stored in an EHR system. It includes all the necessary information to support the clinical decisions and care of the patient over time, may be stored in an Electronic Health Record (EHR) system Or in paper format 4.4- Medical Auditor: A trained professional responsible for conducting medical audits, ensuring accuracy, and providing feedback for compliance and quality improvement. 4.5- Medical Auditing Supervisor: The Medical Auditing Supervisor is responsible for overseeing and coordinating the daily activities of the medical auditing team: This   role ensures that audit processes are performed accurately, efficiently, and in compliance with organizational and regulatory standards 4.6- Medical Auditor Team Leader: The Medical Auditing Team Leader oversees a team of auditors, ensuring accurate and timely completion of audits_ They assign tasks, monitor performance, resolve complex cases, manage incoming requests and communications from billing and authorization teams, and support the onboarding ofnew providers The role focuses on maintaining audit quality, improving processes, and providing guidance and training to the auditing team

4.7- Medical Auditing Manager: The Medical Auditing Team Manager leads and oversees the auditing team, ensuring accurate, efficient, and compliant audit processes  The manager assigns and prioritizes tasks, monitors performance, resolves complex cases, coordinates with other departments, and communicates with senior management and healthcare providers regarding urgent issues. They are responsible for maintaining audit quality, improving processes, and supporting team development. 4.8- Front desk: Helps to confirm   patient   appointments and removes no-show appointments when instructed. 4.9- System Credentials: A username and password assigned to the auditor or healthcare provider, enabling them to log in to the system and access patient   charts,   schedules; and relevant medical documentation 4.10- Click Up: Click Up is cloud-based project management and productivity platform used to plan; track, and manage tasks, projects, and team workflows. It allows teams to organize work; assign tasks, monitor progress, set priorities, and ensure deadlines are met efficiently: 4.11- SOAP Notes: method of   documenting   patient   information using four   categories: Subjective, Objective, Assessment; and Plan: It is a widely used format for medical documentation Subjective: The patient's reported symptoms, history, and experiences_ Objective: Measurable data such as physical exams, test results, and observations_ Assessment: The healthcare provider'$ analysis of the situation, including diagnoses. Plan: The treatment or actions to be taken; including therapy goals and interventions. 4.12- Dx: Diagnosis: The identification of a medical condition based on symptoms, signs, and tests_ 4.13- ICD: International Classification of Diseases, & coding system for diagnoses and conditions. 4.14- CPT: Current Procedural Terminology A set of codes used to describe medical and diagnostic services_

6_ Procedures: 61- Medical Note Verification: 6.1.1- The medical auditing manager assigns auditors to each client Or client's clinic using auditing distribution form code (PS/MA-F-01) 6.1.2- The assigned medical auditor begins  work   after clinic   closure, once all patient documentation is completed by the provider: 6.1.3- Note-Schedule Comparison: compares opened medical notes with the daily schedule to ensure all attending patients have documented notes_ 6.1.4- Missing Notes Handling: If   discrepancies are identified between scheduled appointments and completed medical notes, document the missing notes in the medical tracking sheet and notify the provider to complete them_ 6.1.5- Consultation Notes: If there is any patient consultation, submit it via the designated Google Form code (PSMMA-F-02)_ 6.1.6- The audit is performed in accordance with the criteria outlined in Documentation Tips Appendix, code (PSMMA-A-06)  62_Audit of Completed Notes: 6.2.1- Check accuracy of the following fields for each note: 1. Patient Age/Gender 2_ Date of the note 3_ ICD-10 and Diagnosis 4_ Patient History 5. Chief Complaint 6_ Objectives 7 . Assessment 8_ Problem List & Goals 9_ Plan of Care 10. Billing 6.2.2- The medical auditor checks the internal consistency of all medical notes, and the consistency of the medical note with the patient'$ referral: 6.2.3- Note Finalization: Finalize the completed medical notes under the correct healthcare provider; according to the credentials sheet

6.2.4- Complex Notes Escalation: If a medical note is too complex, escalate to the Senior Medical Auditor; who coordinates with departments and the provider for resolution; with follow-up by the Team Leader and oversight by the Manager Communication via chat emails with provider and billing department: 6.2.5- The audit is performed in accordance with the guidelines outlined in Internal Medical Notes Guidelines code (PSMMA-A-02), as well as the guidelines in Documentation Guidelines code (PSMMA-A-03), and the guidelines in Documentation Tips   code (PSIMA-A-04): 6.3_Double_Review and Finalization Process: 6.3.1- Review of Auditor Work: The Supervisor shall access the audit dashboard to identify audit cases completed by the   assigned   auditor: Each medical note sheet must be   carefully reviewed for completeness, accuracy, and adherence to established documentation standards_ The Supervisor shall also verify that the auditor's comments, justifications, and findings are clearly stated, logically supported, and in full compliance with the organization 's audit guidelines and procedures_ 6.3.2- Verification and Communication: During this stage, the supervisor identifies any pending Or incomplete medical notes that   require further action_ The   supervisor communicates with the   responsible physicians through approved channels such as   email Or the internal chat platform 'Intermedia" to request necessary updates, clarifications, or corrections_ AIl communications   should be tracked and documented to ensure timely  follow-up, response, and closure of outstanding cases in line with departmental timelines 6.3.3- Closure of Audit Notes: Once the physicians   complete  the pending notes,  the Supervisor verifies that all updates conform to documentation standards, compliance criteria, and quality requirements After validation; the Supervisor proceeds to close the audit case in the dashboard system; ensuring that the information is accurate and complete. The closure date is recorded, and the audit status is updated to reflect the final outcome, ensuring traceability and accountability within the audit system:

Internal Medical Notes Guidelines Llf the patient is seen_under 1.1- Direct Access (No Referral) ~ New York State Law: You must perform a reexamination and obtain a referral after: 10 visits OR 30 days, whichever comes first. This is a legal requirement in NY, separate from Medicare or insurance rules.
2-Progress Note: 2.1- Purpose: To document the patient's response to treatment To reflect skilled care and medical necessity Often used to support ongoing treatment and insurance reimbursement 2.2- Timing: Required at least every: 10 treatment visits or every 30 days, whichever comes first (matching Direct Access limits) Can be done more frequently depending on the payer O patient'$ condition
3- Reexamination Note: 3.1- Purpose: To formally reassess the patient after a defined treatment period Required when: There is a significant change in condition There s a discharge O1 need to renew the Plan of Care (POC) 3.2- When to do a formal progress or re-exam note: Progress note: Every 10 visits or 30 days is standard (depending on payer), but if - you're only seeing them monthly, you should consider every visit a "progress" visit in your documentation. Just make it a more detailed Daily note. If the patient comes infrequently (e.g-, once/month), the 90-day rule usually triggers first Reexamination: Required if there is:

4-Plan_of Care Certification/Re-Certification: The initial Plan of Care must be certified by a physician/NPP within 30 days of the first treatment. If therapy continues beyond 90 days; recertification is required (even if the patient hasn 't had 10 visits yet).
5-For_Commercial Insurance (non-Medicare): 5.1- Follow the insurer's specific authorization rules, NOT Medicare rules unless: The insurance requires progress reports every X visits O days: The patient is being seen under a Medicare Advantage Plan which may still follow Medicare-like documentation requirements check the plan).
6 - Discharge Note When Should It Be_Done? 6.1-A discharge note is required when: The goals for the current plan of care (POC) have been met_ The patient is no longer benefiting from skilled PT (plateaued or non-compliant). The patient has stopped attending (e.g-, missed several visits without notice and you re closing the case) The patient is being referred out (e.g , to an ortho or surgeon) The patient completes care for a specific body region Or episode.

Documentation Guidelines for Progress Medical Auditing Department
01.01.2026
Rev Date
00.00.0000
Documentation Guidelines for Progress_Recertification_and Discharge Notes
L Progress Notes Progress notes should be completed on the last authorized session or every 10 sessions, based on the number of visits since the last documented progress note Re-examination notes should be used only when there is a significant change in the patient's condition 2-Recertification Notes_(Medicare Patients) Recertification notes are required every 90 days - from the last one For Medicare patients under Direct Access PT (without physician referral), certification note is required after 10 visits O within the first month, whichever comes first: If the patient has not attended for a month O more, progress note is not required due to the gap in attendance. A detailed daily note is sufficient unless: The patient's authorization has expired The Medicare Plan of Care (POC) has expired A recertification is required 3-Progress_Notes_for Extended Authorizations When insurance approves 16,20, or more sessions, progress notes should be completed every 10 sessions, and again on the last visit of the authorized period. 4- Discharge_Notes 4.1-A discharge note should be completed in any of the following situations: The patient will begin treatment for a new body part (the current case should be discharged to allocate visits to the new case) The patient chooses to end treatment voluntarily The patient has no remaining authorized visits, and authorization will not be renewed so they request last visit of an current authorization as a discharge note 5-New Patients _(Especially Medicare) For all new patients especially Medicare patients verify that the body part documented in the note matches the diagnosis (ICD-10) stated in the referral found in eDocs within the patient's chart This ensures compliance and supports accurate documentation for billing and medical necessity:

Documentation Tips
LCommon Mistakes_to Avoid 1.1 Documentation Errors Failing to   provide   complete and detailed notes (missing   exercises, dosage, patient response). Inadequate progress reports (missing objective changes or justification for continued care) Not updating the POC or recertifying within 90 days ' (Medicare rule): Overuse of EMR templates without customization to the patient: Forgetting to document skilled need and medical necessity: Skipping reassessments intervals (e.g;, every 10 visits).
2 - Billing and Coding Errors 2.1- Upcoding without appropriate documentation to support higher complexity: 2.2- Failing to use CQ (PTA) o CO (OTAF modifiers when applicable:

Execute claim filtration Take Corrective Action Based on the Issue. Collection Agents Record Actions Taken Monitor and Track Progress Supervise filtration accuracy Collection Seniors Monitor follow-up Prevent Recurring Issues 6_ Procedures: 6.1- Filtration Process Identifying Claims for Collection 6.1.1- At the beginning of each month, the collection senior initiates the filtration process using the eligibility sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-0L) This   step is  triggered by the monthly review cycle and the need to identify unpaid or denied claims The agent filters out paid or adjusted claims and highlights those pending beyond 45 days; denied, or missing data. The filtered list is transferred to the Collection Sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-02) for corrective action. 6.2- Take Corrective Action Based on the Issue. 6.2.1- After claims are identified, the collection agent reviews each case to determine the reason for non-payment. Based on the issue, appropriate action is taken such as resubmitting corrected claim, filing a dispute, or updating COB information. Each action is documented in the collection sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-02), including reference numbers or confirmation emails_ Once actions are complete, updates are communicated to the collection senior and billing lead for tracking and verification: 6.3- Monitor and Track Progress_ 6.3.1- Following corrective actions, collection agents monitor the progress of each claim through  medical  portals and payer updates in COB Account Data   Sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-03). They update   claim   statuses   (Pending, Resolved, Denied) in the collection sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-02) after   every   follow-up.  If no update   is received within the expected response time, follow-up calls, O portal checks are made_

I understand that this allows the healthcare provider to offer the appointment slot to another patient in need of care: 2.No-Show Policy: I understand that failing to attend a scheduled appointment without prior notification constitutes a "no-show: Iacknowledge the disruptive impact of no-shows on the healthcare provider's schedule and the ability to provide timely care to other patients. 3. Consequences:
-First Late Cancellation/No-Show: I understand that for the first instance of a no show, a reminder will be issued, and no charges will apply: ASubsequent Late Cancellation/No-Shows: I understand that for subsequent instances of no-shows, fees may be charged to my account as below, and my future scheduling may be subject to review. Cupping Consent Form
Cupping therapy is a form of alternative medicine in which a therapist puts special cups on your skin for a few minutes to create suction. It is used for many purposes, including to help with pain , inflammation blood flow relaxation and well-being and as a type of deep-tissue massage. There is almost no risk of use and is similar to any other form of soft tissue work (i.e. massage or Garston) However, there may be risk of discoloration where the cups were placed This is a voluntarily treatment option they can be accessed by your therapist with your consent In order to be prepared in the event of this treatment is desired by your therapist you are receiving this proactively for permission to utilize Cupping Therapy by the PT of The City team member. The patient &parent/guardian must sign this consent form if you agree to its terms and conditions
understand that all Cupping treatments at this facility are therapeutic and rehabilitative in nature: Information has been provided about Cupping Therapy: understand the potential effects as explained by the therapist: has been explained to me by Therapist that there is a possibility of discolorations after the treatment sessions. Iunderstand that the reaction from the Cupping Therapy is discoloration _ and not bruising: Thi $ reaction occurs from the release and clearing of stagnation and toxins from the body: Ifurther understand that these discolorations will dissipate anywhere from a few hours to as long as tw 0 weeks_ Iagree to communicate with my Therapist if there is any discomfort during the Cupping Therap y session.

CONTENTS: 1- Purpose 2- Scope 3- References 4. Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11- Appendices and Forms 1- Purpose: To provide a clear and standardized procedure for managing all employee benefits, ensuring transparency, compliance, and employee satisfaction. 2_Scope: This   procedure  applies to all activities   related to the administration, management; and maintenance of employee benefits within the organization The scope covers the full life cycle of benefits programs; including but not limited to medical insurance, social insurance, leaves, allowances, transportation support, and any other benefits offered as part of the total rewards strategy. 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- labor law 2025 and insurance law no. 148 2019 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1-Employee Benefits: Non-wage compensations provided to employees in addition to their normal salaries O wages (e.g-, insurance, allowances, leaves)_ 4.2- Medical Insurance:

Health coverage provided by the company through contracted insurance provider for employees and eligible dependents. 4.3- Social Insurance: Mandatory  government insurance covering retirement;  disability,  sickness, and other statutory benefits_ 4.4- Benefit Provider: The external party or vendor responsible for offering and managing the benefit service (e.g , insurance company, government office). 4.5- Benefit Cycle: The timeline covering benefit initiation, updates, renewal, and closure within a specific period (e.g , annually) 4.6- Form I: is the official  document used to register new employee with the Social Insurance Authority in Egypt: It notifies the authority that an employment relationship has begun 4.7-Form 6: is the official document used to terminate an employee's registration with the Social Insurance Authority. It notifies the authority that the employment relationship has ended for any reason (resignation, termination, end of contract, death, etc ). 5_Responsibility for_Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes CEO Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes_ HR Manager Verify the confirmation for the employment The Senior HR manages and oversees the entire benefits process_ Generalist Provide the necessary data and make the newcome sign the HR coordinator insurance form and contract

6_ Procedures:
6.1- Eligibility Overview Benefits: 6.1.1- Medical insurance: Eligible after successful completion of the probation period (as stated in the employment contract) 6.1.2- Other allowances   (transportation): As per company policy from the first day of employment_ 6.2- Social Insurance Enrollment: 6.2.1- Submit Form 1 6.2.2- Maintain updated reports of insured employees. 6.2.3- Ensure full compliance with Egyptian labor law _ 6.3- Medical Insurance Enrollment (Post-Probation): 6.3.1- Monitor the employee '$ probation period closely. 6.3.2- Upon the employee signs the contract (code: PS/HR-F-01): 6.3.2.1- Verify the confirmation letter signed by the general manager and HR Manager: 6.3.2.2- Register the employee in the medical insurance list within 10 working days after probation. 6.3.4- Submit the employee's details and any dependent   information to the insurance provider: 6.4- Allowances and Other Benefits: 6.4.1- Review employee s eligibility based on position and department: 6.5- Employee Communication and Support: 6.5.1- Inform new employees during onboarding that medical insurance is activated after probation period. 6.5.2- Respond to benefit inquiries within 2 working days 6.5.3- Collect feedback after activation to evaluate provider performance by email: 6.6- Benefits Termination: 6.6.1- Upon employee resignation or termination: 6.6.1.1- Submit form 6 for social insurance cancellation_

CONTENTS 1- Purpose 2- Scope 3 - References Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures

1- Purpose: This document clarifies the processes followed by the company for creating, modifying and identifying documents, including externally originated  documented information, related to the quality management system This document also describes the processes through which these documents are issued and revised within the company _ 2_Scope: 2.1- The   preparation, issuance, review modification, classification and  identification of documented information within the quality   management   system across all levels (procedures, work instructions/SOPs, forms, etc.) 2.2- The maintenance and updating of externally originated documented information: 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Documented Information: Information that requires control within the company, including the type of medium in which it is contained. 4.2- Procedure: A document that explains how and in what sequence a specific overall process is performed: It is considered a second-level document in the hierarchy of the quality management system documents.

Typically, records are completed forms that require retention_ 4.6- Appendix: An appendix is document that adds  information that  supports   another  document by adding information or data not essential for understanding the process, appendices often include process flowcharts o tables of data_ 4.7- Document Identification and Classification: Defining and distinguishing the document by a unique name and code for each document within the scope of the management system. 4.8- Document: Refers to all copies of maintained documented information, including all revisions_ 5_Responsibility for_Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes_ CEO Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes_ Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this Head of Operational procedure are implemented and described, and for ensuring Excellence that any changes made to controlled documents are reviewed and approved: Responsible for reviewing all documents within the scope of Quality Assurance this procedure to ensure its conformance with standards Manager requirements and reviews its business impact. 6_ Procedures:
6.1- Request Submission: 6.1.1- All support requests must be submitted through the official "Support Request Form" (code: PS/OpEx-F-01) or via the company's internal communication platform Or email.
6.1.2- Requests must include a clear description, objective, expected outcome, and any relevant data or attachments_
6.2- Request Review and Prioritization: 6.2.1- The OpEx team reviews requests within 2.5 business days of submission: 6.2.2- Requests are assessed based on urgency, impact, alignment with strategic priorities, and available capacity. 6.2.3- The head of opex approves o assigns priority levels and the support lead who will works on this task:
6.3- Assignment and Execution: 6.3.1- A support lead is assigned to coordinate the task 6.3.2- The support lead prepares an action plan form (code: PSIOpEx-F-02) or timeline based on the request type. 6.3.3- Progress updates are communicated to the requester periodically. 6.4- Validation and Closure:
6.4.1- Upon completion, the requester reviews and confirms satisfaction. 6.4.2- Documentation, templates, and outcomes are archived in the OpEx knowledge base. 6.4.3- Feedback is collected and recorded for continuous improvement in Monthly Strategic form (code: PS/PM-F-01) 6.5- Escalation and Exception Handling: 6.5.1- Any delay, issue, or deviation from SLA must be reported immediately to the head of OpEx via email: 6.5.2- Corrective or preventive actions are logged and tracked as   part of  quality improvement efforts and will be handled by the quality assurance

4.4- HIPAA: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act: US legislation providing data privacy and security provisions for medical information_ 4.5- SME: Subject Matter Expert person with deep knowledge in specific company service area (e.g,, Medical Billing). 4.6- Pipeline: The end-to-end process from data ingestion and cleaning to model training, deployment, and monitoring: 4.7-Bias: Systematic error in model that causes it to produce inaccurate Or unfair results across different groups 4.8- Client: Any individual, group, Or organization that receives a product, service, Or specific output that is intended for or required by the client. client can be internal to the company or external to it. 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation:

Provides and maintains the necessary infrastructure and IT Department deployment environment for the AI solutions_ Department Heads Responsible for identifying business problems, providing SMEs, (Service Delivery) validating solution performance, and ensuring adoption Data Governance Ensures data used for training and testing is compliant with Officer HIPAA.

6- Procedures: 6.1- Problem Identification & Feasibility 6.1.1- Idea Generation & Intake (Form PSIAI-F-01) Business needs are captured via a formal request o identified during strategy meetings. The request details the problem; business impact, and potential Al value 6.1.2- Initial Assessment AI Lead and relevant SME evaluate technical feasibility, data availability/quality, compliance risks, and alignment with strategic goals. 6.1.3- Decision Gate 1 (Go/No-Go) AI Manager and relevant department head approve proceeding to POC. 6.2- Proof of Concept (POC) & Design 6.2.1- Data Acquisition & Preparation Data Scientists work with the Data Governance Officer to securely acquire and clean representative, HIPAA-compliant dataset 6.2.2- Model Exploration & POC AI Engineers/Data Scientists rapidly develop and test initial model architectures_ Focus is on demonstrating the core hypothesis that can be solved with AI: 6.2.3- Architecture Design Based on successful POC, detailed architecture and system design documents are created_ including integration points and required infrastructure. 6.2.4- Decision Gate 2 (POC Approval) AI Manager and IT approve the POC, confirming infrastructure readiness and resource allocation: 6.3- Development & Iteration 6.3.1- Full Data Pipeline Development The complete, production-ready data ingestion, transformation, and storage pipeline is built. 6.3.2- Model Training & Optimization Models are rigorously trained, optimized, and tested against defined performance metrics, and simultaneously evaluated for bias with initial validation performed to ensure consistency and alignment with the defined inputs and input specifications. 6.3.3- Internal Testing & Verification The solution is tested for functionality, performance, and robustness by the Al and IT teams This includes extensive back-testing against _ historical data and verification that the solution behaves correctly and consistently with the defined inputs and input specifications

6.4- Pilot & Deployment 6.4.1- Pilot Preparation The solution is integrated into a controlled, non-critical production environment: 6.4.2- Pilot Execution (MVP Launch) The solution is deployed as an MVP to a small, isolated group of users or for a specific service line for the client (e.g , a subset of Medical Auditing cases) 6.4.3- User Feedback & Performance Monitoring Continuous monitoring and collection of user feedback are performed. Model performance is tracked against real-time operational metrics. It is recorded in AI Piloting form code PS/AI-F-02 6.4.4 Change Requests Documentation: Any change requested by the client during the pilot phase must be formally documented using the same request form code (PS/AI-F-01), completed as a new change-request sheet and attached to the original request form code (PS/AI-F-01) 6.4.5- Decision Gate 3 (Production Readiness) Successful pilot completion, approval by the Al Manager and the relevant client. 6.5- Maintenance & Improvement 6.5.1- Ongoing Monitoring A dedicated team monitors model performance for Drift (degradation in prediction accuracy over time) and system health. It is recorded in AI Outputs Monitoring Form code (PSIAI-F-04): 6.5.2- Retraining & Updates Model retraining may be initiated as decision resulting from the monitoring activities. When this is the case, it is documented on the AI Monitoring Plan Form code (PSIAI-F-03) 6.5.3- Feature Enhancement New feature requests and improvement iterations feed back into Process 1, ensuring the solution evolves with clients and Our partners' needs_

1 Purpose: The purpose of this procedure is to establish a structured virtual field operations process that provides continuous remote supervision of clinic operations from opening to closing: This procedure ensures real-time operational, quality, and compliance control, enables proactive identification and resolution of issues, supports data-driven performance monitoring and reporting, and defines clear escalation and follow-up mechanisms across all involved departments _ 2-Scope: This procedure applies to all RED Agents responsible for remote clinic oversight and covers monitoring and support of clinic operational readiness and opening compliance; patient flow processes including check-in, check-out, next appointment scheduling, authorizations, and patient surveys; Virtual Front Desk and PCC non-clinical performance impacting clinic operations; staff presence, schedule adherence, and coverage; safety, regulatory, and visual HIPAA compliance; coordination of vendors, maintenance, facilities, and logistics; and tracking clinic performance KPIs with identification and escalation of performance deviations_ 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)

6.2- Training Needs Analysis (TNA): 6.2.1- The assigned L&D Supervisor performs detailed analysis of the request to confirm the performance gap, target audience and define clear: 6.2.2- The L&D Supervisor consults with relevant Subject Matter Experts SMEs) and the requesting manager to validate the need and scope. 6.23- The L&D Manager  coordinates with Human Resources to identify the training programs the company needs to implement_ 6.3- Training Design & Development: 6.3.1- Based on the TNA (code:   PSIL&D-F-02) and  taking   into consideration each employee 's individual training plan form (code: PS/L&D-F-03) if applicable, the L&D supervisor coordinates with the relevant department manager to determine whether the   training can be delivered   internally by the department_ If internal delivery is not feasible, the L&D supervisor coordinates with certified external training provider, which will be responsible for developing the training materials and delivering the training: 6.3.2- The L&D supervisor   shall evaluate the external training   provider in training providers evaluation form (code: PS/L&D-F-04) 6.4- Training Scheduling & Communication: 6.4.1- The L&D supervisor schedules the   training   session in coordination with the department head to minimize disruption to operational workflow 6.4.2- Calendar   invitations and communication are sent to all participants   and their managers with details of the training: It recorded in the training calendar  form (code: PS/L&D-F-0S) 6.5- Training Delivery: 6.5.1- The L&D supervisor Or an approved trainer delivers the training as planned. 6.5.- Attendance is  mandatory and recorded on the training attendance record (code: PS/L&D-F-06). 6.5.3- In case of non-attendance, The L&D Supervisor notifies the HR department to take the necessary action with the employee:
