CONTENTS 1- Purpose 2- Scope 3- References 4_ Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11- Appendices and Forms 1 Purpose: The purpose of this procedure is to ensure that all medical services rendered are authorized in compliance with insurance requirements. This  process  safeguards against claim  denials, supports accurate billing; and promotes effective coordination between clinical, administrative, and billing departments. It establishes standardized workflow for verifying eligibility, obtaining prior authorizations, monitoring active approvals, and resolving denials or expired authorizations_ 2-Scope: 2.1- This procedure applies to all staff involved in the authorization workflow, including the authorization department; client relations team, therapists/clinical staff, and credentialing department_ 2.2-It covers all stages of the authorization process from patient eligibility verification and submission of prior authorization requests to monitoring approvals, renewals, and denial management for all   patients receiving treatment under commercial, workers compensation (WC), Or no-fault (NF) insurance: 3- References; 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- External Regulatory and Industry References: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) ICD-10-CM, CPT, and HCPCS Coding Standards Payer-specific Authorization Guidelines

4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Authorization: Official approval from an insurance company confirming that the proposed treatment O service is covered under the patient'$ plan: 4.2- Eligibility and Benefits Check: The process of verifying a patient's active insurance coverage and plan benefits_ 4.3- Initial Evaluation: The first therapy session   during which the patient' $ condition is assessed and documented. 4.4- Plan of Care (POC): treatment plan created by the therapist that defines the frequency, duration, and type of services to be rendered_ 4.5- Prior Approval: A requirement by some payers to obtain authorization before providing certain services 4.6- Patient Filtration: daily review process to confirm that all patients who attended the clinic have valid authorizations for their sessions_ 4.7-Re-Evaluation: follow-up assessment documenting patient progress and justifying continued care O additional sessions_ 4.8- Credentialing Information: Provider details (NPI, Tax ID, location) necessary for accurate authorization submission: 4.9- Medical Necessity Denial: rejection issued by an insurer indicating insufficient justification for treatment based on medical evidence. 4.10- Peer-to-Peer Review: discussion between the provider and an insurance medical reviewer to advocate for approval after a denial. 4.11-EMR System: The digital system used for documentation, scheduling, and record management 4.12- CPT and ICD-I0 Codes: Standard  codes used to describe procedures and  diagnoses for insurance and billing purposes_

Title
Responsibility
Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes. Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes_ Oversees the authorization process, ensures compliance with payer   policies, and resolves escalated or urgent cases Conducts daily patient filtration submits prior authorization requests, monitors approvals _ manages denials, and maintains accurate records in the EMR_ Verifies insurance eligibility and  provides   up-to-date patient insurance details to the authorization department: Provide clinical documentation such as evaluations, POCs, and progress notes and schedule history required to justify authorizations. Ensures   providers and locations are in-network with insurance plans and resolves credentialing discrepancies Maintains login sheets and credentialing plan updates. Coordinates with the authorization department to align approved authorizations with submitted claims and manage denial corrections

6_ Procedures: 6.1- Patient Filtration: Each day, the authorization specialist reviews the clinic's schedule for the previous day to ensure that all patients who attended have valid authorizations for services rendered 6.1.1- For clinics using WebPT: The list of attended patients is extracted directly from WebPT (code: PS/B&A-F-04). Patients requiring authorizations are added to the external clients combined authorization sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-07), which centralizes all WebPT-based authorization tracking; 6.1.22- For clinics using other EMR   systems: The authorization specialist  fills the submission sheet authorization subsheet (code: PS/B&A-F-08) with the patient names and dates of service from the previous day. Patients requiring authorization are then monitored from the authorization tab within the submission sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-08) This approach is used because non-WebPT systems lack efficient authorization tracking tools like Reminder Mate (code: PSIB&A-F-0S) and Dr Chrono (code: PSIB&A-F-06). 6.1.3- The   specialist   then reviews each  patient's authorization status (valid,  expired, missing, or pending renewal) Any missing O expired authorizations are flagged for follow-up and request initiation. 6.2- Requesting Prior Approvals: When authorizations are required, the authorization specialist   gathers and submits requests based on insurance requirements and clinical documentation. 6.2.1- Trigger: When a patient ' evaluation Or treatment requires prior authorization, O an existing authorization is near expiry within the last 24 hours. 6.2.2- Action: Verify plan   requirements for initial evaluations, re-evaluations, or expired authorizations   through  the department  reference or   insurance communication if needed. Gather patient demographics, and clinical notes (evaluation, POC, progress reports) from EMR system: , credentialing data from login sheet (code: PSICRE-F-05) Confirm ICD-10 and CPT codes. Submit the request via fax, portal, or phone, depending on payer guidelines.

6.2.3- Output: Authorization request successfully submitted with tracking/reference number: 6.2.4- Communication: Updates recorded in the EMR and Authorization/Submission Sheet; relevant stakeholders (therapist, billing, client) notified if required. 6.3- Monitoring and Follow-Up: After   submission, each authorization request is monitored by the authorization specialist daily until a decision is received. 6.3.1- Review response timeframes (typically 0-10 business days) 6.3.2- Check portal for status updates. 6.3.3- For payers without portals, contact via phone after the expected processing window. 6.3.4- Update EMR and submission/authorization sheet with the latest status (Pending, Approved, Denied, Partially Approved). 6.35- Notify therapists and billing of approvals o denials_ 6.4- Managing Denials and Expired Authorizations: If an authorization is denied, expired, or partially approved, the authorization specialist investigates and resolves the issue promptly. Common scenarios include: 6.4.1- Active approval with another provider: Submit patient consent (code: PS/B&A-F- 09) or discharge note. 6.4.2- Plan limitations met: Verify benefit renewal dates_ 6.4.3- Missing  documentation: Request and attach missing  notes or   forms from the client/medical auditing team according to the client's contract 6.4.4- Credentialing  issues: Coordinate with  credentialing   department  via  ClickUp in credentialing inquiries form (code: PSMB&A-F-l0). 6.4.5- Medical necessity denial: Initiate a peer-to-peer review if applicable. 6.4.6- All denial resolutions are recorded in the EMR and   submission sheet (code: PSMB&A-F-08)_ 6.5- Recordkeeping and Reporting: 6.5.1- Maintain updated records of all authorizations, pending requests, and resolutions in the tracking sheet comments for additional notes_ 6.5.2- Keep copies of evaluation reports, POCs, referrals, and fax confirmations in shared folders_ 6.5.3- Coordinate with Billing to ensure alignment between authorizations and submitted claims

LPurpose: To provide structured process   for welcoming and integrating new employees  into the organization_ 2-Scope: This procedure applies to all employees involved in the onboarding and offboarding processes across the organization: It covers the activities, roles, and responsibilities related to employee joining and separation, including coordination between HR, direct managers IT, and other   supporting   departments to ensure proper access setup, documentation, asset handover, and system deactivation in accordance with company policies 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- labor law 2025 and insurance law no. 148 2019. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Employee Benefits: Refers to all non-wage compensations provided to employees, including social insurance, medical insurance, allowances, and other approved perks.

6.4- System and Benefits Registration: 6.4.1- HR Coordinator inputs new hire data into HR system. 6.4.2- Senior HR Generalist Register employee in payroll, medical insurance _ 6.4.3- Verify activation of all accounts and benefits_ 6.5- Follow-Up and Feedback: 6.5.1- HR coordinator checks within one week to ensure all documents are complete_ Note: This procedure shall be reviewed annually by the HR department Or when process changes occur: 6.6- Offboarding Process (Employee Exit Process): 6.6.1- Initiation of Offboarding: 6.6.1.1- The offboarding process is initiated when an employee submits resignation form receives termination decision, reaches the end of their contract, O in cases of death or medical incapacity. 6.6.1.2- Resignation approval is obtained through the line manager signature on the resignation form, which is then sent via email to HR by the employee o the direct manager_ 6.6.1.3- The HR specialist schedule an exit meeting with the employee: 6.6.1.4- The HR specialist notify relevant departments (IT, Admin; Finance, Payroll, Security, etc. via email. 6.6.2- Documentation & Compliance: 6.6.2.1- The HR Specialist collects all required documents, including the resignation form or termination decision; clearance and asset return form and exit interview form (code: PS/HR-F-02)_ 6.6.2.2- The HR Specialist   prepares the   official  forms, including Form (Social Insurance Termination Form), updates the employee' $ status on HRIS, and prepares the final payroll details 6.6.2.3- The Department Manager is responsible for ensuring the full handover of tasks and pending work, access permissions and files; and project documents

6.6.2.4- The Department Manager collects all company-owned items from the employee, including laptop or mobile, ID card, access cards, keys; and any tools or equipment in coordination with IT department: 6.6.2.5- The employee, the department manager;, IT department and HR all sign the clearance and assets return form (code: PSIHR-F-03) and submit it to HR_ 6.6.2.6- The IT Department is responsible for disabling access to the employee 's email, HR systems, ERPICRM systems, and internal communication tools, backing up essential work files, and confirming the closure of all accounts 6.6.3- Clearance & Final Settlement 6.6.3.1- The HR Specialist processes the final salary; including unpaid salary, leave encashment; deductions (loans, penalties, etc.) , and benefits settlement 6.6.3.2- The HR Specialist issues HR letter (Code: PS/HR-F-04) (if applicable) and the clearance and assets  return   form (code: PS/HR-F-03) receipt signed by the employee: 6.6.3.3- The HR Specialist submits Form 6 to the Social Insurance Authority, removes the employee from the medical insurance list, and archives all exit documents in the personnel file_ 6.6.3.4- The Senior HR Specialist conducts the exit interview, discussing the reasons for leaving and collecting feedback on the work environment; leadership, HR processes, and company culture_ 6.6.3.5- The HR Specialist confirms that all relevant departments have signed off on the clearance and assets return form (code: PSIHR-F-03)_ 6.6.3.6- The employee receives the final settlement, HR letter Code: PSIHR-F-04) and any other required certificates or documents_ 7-Revision Amendment Log: Revision # Date Changes 01.01.2026 Document Creation_

CONTENTS: 1- Purpose 2- Scope 3 - References Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11- Appendices and Forms 1- Purpose: The objective of this SOP is to outline the process for managing demands and requests within the Service Delivery Department at COB Solution: This includes: Defining the difference between demands (long-term service needs) and requests (short-term, specific inquiries) Establishing structured workflow for handling demands and requests across various departments (Billing, Authorization;, Credentialing; Medical Auditing, and Customer Care)_ Ensuring timely, accurate, and efficient resolution of inquiries to maintain operational efficiency and customer satisfaction 2- Scope: This SOP applies to all activities related to the management of demands and requests within the Service Delivery Department at COB Solution. It covers: Receiving, categorizing, and resolving demands and requests _ Coordination between internal teams (Billing, Authorization; Credentialing; Medical Auditing, and Customer Care). Documentation and communication protocols for all inquiries 3-References: The International Standard ISO 9001.2015.

6.2- Process for Demands and Requests in Credentialing: 6.2.1- Receiving and Tracking Requests: Log credentialing requests (new enrollments, re-credentialing, provider updates) Validate and categorize requests, flagging urgent cases for priority handling: 6.2.2- Monitoring Progress: Follow up with the Credentialing Team to check the status of pending applications Escalate delays and ensure compliance with payer requirements_ 6.2.3- Resolving Issues: Verify completion of credentialing and update all systems. Notify stakeholders and close the request 6.3- Process for Demands and Requests in Medical Auditing 6.3. 1-Receiving and Logging Audit Requests from Clients: The Account Manager receives the inquiry directly from the client via email or call (provider, clinic, or designated contact)_ The Account Manager reviews the inquiry, confirms the details, and logs - the request in ClickUp. The Account Manager assigns the request to the Medical Auditing Team for assessment and action_ 6.3.2- Documentation Review Report Preparation (Medical Auditing Team)= Once assigned, the Medical Auditing Team reviews all relevant medical records, coding entries, and documentation_ The team identifies all documentation gaps, coding issues, and compliance discrepancies_ The Medical Auditing Team is fully responsible for preparing, finalizing, and issuing the complete audit report; including: Findings Required corrections Root causes Or patterns Preventive and corrective action recommendations The finalized report is shared with providers, the Billing Team, and relevant stakeholders _

6.3.3- Resolution Closure: The Account Manager coordinates with providers and billing staff to ensure all recommended corrections are completed. The Medical Auditing Team verifies the corrections if needed The Account Manager updates the inquiry status in ClickUp and closes the request once resolved_ 6.4- Process for Demands and Requests in Customer Care: 6.4.1- Receiving and Logging Customer Care Inquiries The Account Manager receives the inquiry directly from the client (clinic, provider; or designated representative) The Account Manager reviews the inquiry, clarifies missing details, and logs - the request in ClickUp. The Account Manager assigns the request to the Customer Care Team for handling 6.4.2- Handling and Resolving Customer Care Inquiries (Customer Care Team): Once assigned, the Customer Care Team reviews the request and validates details in the EMR or other systems. The Customer Care Team is fully responsible for resolving all patient-related operational inquiries, including: Appointment scheduling or confirmation Patient communication follow-ups EMR navigation Or front-desk workflow support The Customer Care Team documents all actions taken and updates the inquiry status in ClickUp. If additional clarification or clinic action is required, the team coordinates accordingly. 6.4.3- Resolution & Closure: The Customer Care Team notifies the Account Manager once action is completed. The Account Manager reviews and confirms that the inquiry has been fully resolved. The  Account   Manager closes the   request in ClickUp and updates   the client   if applicable

1 Purpose: The purpose of this document is to provide clear and detailed procedure for submitting medical claims to insurance companies It outlines the steps necessary to ensure accurate and timely submission of claims 2-Scope: 2.1- This procedure applies to all administrative and billing- staff, medical coders, and insurance claim processors responsible for preparing and submitting medical claims on behalf of the practice or facility. It defines the boundaries of claim submission activities, ensuring consistency and compliance across all billing operations 2.2 - The procedure covers the entire process of claim submission from gathering and verifying patient information to submitting  claims  through the appropriate electronic platforms  (such as Therabill, Webpt billing Jopari, etc ) It ensures  that all required documentation, authorizations, and   coding modifiers are accurately included. This procedure applies to all types of claims, including  Commercial  Insurance, Workers " Compensation WC), and No-Fault (NF) claims_

3- References:
3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- External Regulatory and Industry References: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) Compliance for secure handling of patient health and billing information CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) Guidelines For claims submission requirements, billing codes, and modifier usage ICD-I0-CM, CPT, and HCPCS Coding Standards Official medical coding systems required for claim accuracy: Payer-specific   Billing Guidelines Including Commercial   Insurance, Workers Compensation (WC), and No-Fault (NF) carrier rules_ 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- EMR (Electronic Medical Record): digital version of a patient's medical chart containing clinical and billing information used for claim generation and tracking: 4.2- Practice Mate: The practice management and billing system used to create, process, and submit claims electronically to the clearinghouse o payer: 4.3- CPT Codes (Current Procedural Terminology): Standardized codes used to describe medical, surgical, and diagnostic services for billing and insurance claim purposes. 4.4- ICD-10 Codes (International Classification of Diseases; 1Oth Revision): Codes used to classify and report medical diagnoses and reasons for patient visits in claims processing: 4.5-CMS-1500 Form: The standard paper claim form used by healthcare providers to bill insurance carriers for services rendered. 4.6- UN (Unauthorized): Indicates that a claim or service was rendered without the required authorization O pre- approval from the insurance payer

4.7- Missing (Missing Note): Refers to the absence of required clinical documentation O notes needed to support the claim submission 4.8- Clinical Note: A detailed record written by the healthcare provider that documents the patient' S encounter treatment, and progress. It serves as the primary source of medical_ justification for billing and claim submission_ 4.9- Date of Service (DOS): The specific date on which a healthcare service was provided to the patient: 4.10- Modifiers: Two-digit codes added to CPT or HCPCS codes to provide additional information about the service performed, without changing its definition: 4.11- Login Sheet: An internal document containing each clinic'$ specific information; including address, Tax ID, and insurance portal login credentials, used for claim submissions_ 4.12- Credentialing Plan: document containing the client' s credentialing   information with various   insurance payers, ensuring accurate setup and claim routing; 4.13- Submission Sheet: A record sheet that tracks all claim submissions for each client; including submission dates, payer names, and claim status_ 4.14- Clearinghouse: A_ third-party   intermediary that electronically   transmits healthcare claims from the provider's   billing   system to the insurance payer: It validates claim data;, ensures compliance with payer requirements, and returns acceptance Or rejection reports_ 4.15- Clearinghouse Rejection: An error response generated by the clearinghouse when submitted claim fails initial validation checks due to missing or incorrect data_ 4.16- Commercial Insurance: A type of health insurance provided by private companies that covers medical services for policyholders. Claims   under  commercial   insurance must   include accurate policy and authorization details as per payer requirements_

4.17- No-Fault Insurance (NF): Insurance coverage that pays for medical treatment and related expenses after an accident; regardless of who was at fault: Claims under No-Fault require accident details, Assignment of Benefits (AOB) forms, and referral documentation. 4.18- Workers' Compensation Insurance (WC): Insurance that provides coverage for employees injured on the job, including medical treatment and wage replacement: WC claims must include accident information; body site details, and valid date of injury. 5-Responsibility for Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes. CEO Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes _ Responsible for submitting claims to insurance companies on behalf  of the   practice or facility. This includes   gathering Claim Submission accurate patient information;  reviewing CPT codes and Team modifiers, and ensuring proper documentation is attached to the claims Ensures that the provider and location are appropriate for the Credentialing specific insurance plan being submitted  They also handle the Department enrollment of each   provider with the relevant insurance companies to ensure eligibility and proper claim submission. Authorization Responsible for obtaining authorization for each date of service Department when required. They ensure that proper authorization is secured Ensures the accuracy and completeness of medical Medical Auditing documentation, including progress notes They are responsible Department for confirming that all required notes are included, finalized, and properly closed after the provider has completed their part

6_ Procedures: 6.1- Filling the Submission Records: The claim submission process begins with the preparation of the clinic submission sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-12) , which serves as the primary tracker for all claims_ This step starts the day after the Date of Service (DOS), once all appointments for that date have been finalized in the schedule. The Billing Specialist opens the Submission Sheet and enters each patient' S name, date of service. 6.1.1- Access the clinic Submission Sheet from the COB Account Data Sheet code: PSIB&A-F-03). 6.1.2- Cross-check the appointment schedule to ensure all encounters from the DOS are captured. 6.1.3- Record the patient's name, Date of Service 6.1.4- Highlight or tag any encounters missing documentation, unauthorized sessions_ Note: For clinics utilizing any EMR different than WEBPT, filling the clinic submission sheet is the responsibility of the authorization agent handling the client; please refer to the authorization procedure_ This ensures all encounters are accounted for before billing begins, minimizing missed or delayed submissions Output:   Complete  therapy  appointments records of the previous day in the clinic submission sheet for internal review 6.2- Prepare Patient Information: Once the clinic submission sheet is ready, the Billing Specialist verifies the accuracy of all patient and provider details before initiating claim creation: 6.2.1- Open the patient '$ chart in the EMR system (e.g-, Webpt) (code: PS/B&A-F-1l) 6.2.2- Confirm patient identifiers first name, last name, date of birth; insurance ID, Address match the chart and eligibility record. 6.2.3- Verify that the referring provider is listed on the chart if needed (According to payer'$ specific rules)_ 6.2.4- Confirm the rendering provider's credentialing status using the credentialing plan sheet (code: PSICRE-F-09) and Login Sheet (code: PSICRE-F-05). 6.2.5- Correct any discrepancies in the above by coordinating with client relations or the clinic Google space. Ensuring all data is correct at this stage avoids claim  rejections due to invalid demographic O provider information:

Accurate insurance verification ensures the claim is routed properly to the payer and avoids processing delays. 6.4- Review CPT Codes and Modifiers: After confirming insurance details, the Billing Specialist reviews all clinical documentation to assign correct CPT codes and necessary modifiers 6.4.1- Access progress Or medical notes for the patient in the EMR system (code: PS/B&A- F-13). 6.4.2- Identify and match CPT codes to documented services in Medical Note form code: PSIB&A-F-13) 6.4.3- Review ICD-10 codes to ensure diagnosis alignment with the procedure performed. 6.4.4- Report any missing information to the client or the auditing team according to the client' s contract This ensures clinical accuracy and compliance with payer rules, reducing denial risk: 6.5- Fill Out the CMS-1500 Form: Once the claim has been coded and verified, the Billing Specialist completes the CMS-1500 form in Practice Mate or the designated software_ 6.5.1- Open the CMS-1500 template and enter patient demographics, insurance details, and provider information_ 6.5.2- Input the Date of Service, CPT codes, ICD-10 codes, and modifiers_ 6.5.3- Include the referring provider (if applicable), authorization number (if applicable), and accident details for WCINF claims_ 6.5.4- Review each field for completeness and accuracy. Accurate completion of the CMS-1500 form ensures the claim passes clearinghouse and payer validation

6.6- Submit the Claim: Once the claim has been reviewed and approved, the Claim Submission Specialist proceeds to finalize and transmit it through the billing system: Using the designated submission platform, the  specialist   selects  the completed claim and presses the (Transmit Claim button to submit it electronically to the clearinghouse Or payer: After submission, the specialist immediately updates the clinic submission sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-12) with the submission date_ This ensures that all transmitted claims are accurately logged and traceable for follow-up, reconciliation, and reporting: 6.6.1: Open the patient's claim within the applicable billing software. 6.6.2: Review one final time for accuracy and ensure all required attachments (referral o authorization) are included if applicable. 6.6.3: Press the ~Transmit Claim button to send the claim   electronically to the clearinghouse or payer 6.6.4: Once transmitted, record the submission date and the submitting agent code in the clinic submission sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-12). This simplified process ensures quick, accurate, and traceable claim submission with minimal manual intervention while maintaining proper documentation control. 6.7- Monitoring and Updating Submission Sheet: After submission, the Billing Specialist monitors the claim status and updates the clinic submission sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-l2) to reflect current progress: 6.7.1- Access the transmits claim dashboard (code: PSIB&A-F-14) to check the submission status of each claim_ 6.7.2- Review clearinghouse responses daily for any rejections O errors. 6.7.3- For rejected claims, identify the reason (e.g;, invalid payer ID, missing modifier; incomplete information) in Rejection Reason Panel Form (code: PS/B&A-F-15) 6.7.4- Correct the issue in Practice Mate, resubmit the claim, and update the clinic submission sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-12) with the new submission date Note: The interface and steps for filling " out the CMS-1500 form and transmitting the claim may vary slightly depending on the version Or configuration of Practice Mate or the billing platform used. However; all systems follow the same standard procedure outlined above completing the claim form accurately, verifying all required details, and transmitting the claim for payer processing:

Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms L- Purpose: The objective of this procedure is to establish clear; consistent, and structured approach to the recruitment and selection process within the organization. It aims to ensure the timely acquisition of qualified and competent candidates in alignment with the company'$ strategic workforce needs, while promoting fairness, transparency, and compliance with legal and organizational standards. 2- Scope: 2.1- This procedure applies to all recruitment and selection activities conducted within the organization, covering full-time, part-time, temporary, and contractual positions across all departments and business units_ 2.2 - The scope includes the end-to-end hiring process from  manpower planning and job requisition, through sourcing and interviewing; to final selection, offer issuance, and onboarding coordination: 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- labor law 2025 and insurance law no. 148 2019 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- SOP: Standard Operating Procedure 4.2- SOP Owner: The author of the SOP 4.3- QA: Quality Assurance

5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility The role is responsible for developing and executing recruitment strategies aligned with organizational goals, leading and mentoring the recruitment team; and collaborating with department heads to identify staffing needs and define job requirements_ It oversees candidate   sourcing   through HR Manager multiple  channels, tracks and reports key recruitment  metrics, ensures compliance with legal, organizational, and diversity standards, manages external recruitment partners when needed, approves job offers and salary packages, and ensures all candidate documentation is properly collected and organized. The role is responsible for drafting and publishing job advertisements in coordination with hiring managers, sourcing and screening candidates Talent through various platforms, conducting initial interviews and assessments, Acquisition and maintaining accurate records in the ATS. It also coordinates interview Specialist scheduling and feedback, participates in candidate evaluations, provides timely feedback to the TA team; collaborates with HR on  final hiring decisions, and supports the job offer and onboarding processes_ The role supports the recruitment process by assisting in posting job advertisements scheduling interviews, and communicating with HR candidates. It helps prepare job offers, manages accepted applicants, and coordinator assists with the onboarding process, including coordinating equipment and system access_

6_ Procedures:
6.1- Workforce Planning Job Requisition: 6.1.1- Department head identifies the need for a new hire OT replacement: 6.1.2- Hiring manager   requests   hiring via email. The email must  include job  title, department, salary range, and justification: 6.1.3- HR manager reviews and approves the requisition via email: 6.1.4- HR manager responsible for doing job analysis (code: PS/HR-F-0S) for each job. 6.2- Job Description Preparation: 6.2.1- Talent acquisition specialist coordinates with the hiring manager to draft o update the job description (code: PS/HR-F-06). 6.2.2- JD includes job title, responsibilities, qualifications, skills, and reporting structure. 6.3- Job Posting: 63.1- The TA specialist posts the job on internal and external platforms (eg , Internal (Via Email), LinkedIn; Wuzzuf, social media etc.) 6.3.2- Job is also   shared  via   employee   referral   programs or   recruitment agencies, if applicable 6.4- Sourcing & Sereening: 6.4.1- TA Specialist actively sources candidates using job boards, ATS, and professional networks_
6.4.2- Applications are screened based on JD criteria. 6.4.3- Shortlisted candidates are contacted for an initial phone or video screening: 6.5- Interview Process:
6.5.1- Upon arrival, the interviewee fills out the application form (code: PSMHR-F-07). 6.5.2- First-round interviews conducted by TA_ 6.5.3- Second-round technical/managerial interviews conducted by the hiring manager and/or relevant team leads. 6.5.4- Final interviews may include upper management or panel interviews depending on the position level:

Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms 1- Purpose: The purpose of this policy is to establish structured and transparent escalation process that ensures timely and effective resolution of issues, incidents, and concerns across all COB departments_ This policy aims to enhance accountability, support decision-making, and foster culture of open and responsible communication: 2-Scope: This escalation policy applies to all COB employees, across all locations and departments, including full-time, part-time, temporary, and outsourced staff: The policy ensures that all concerns, conflicts, operational issues, Or misconduct are handled through structured and hierarchical communication process to promote accountability,  transparency, and  timely resolution: 3_References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 3.2- labor law 2025 and insurance law no. 148 2019 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Escalation: Refers to the process of raising an issue, concern, or incident to a higher authority Or management level when: The matter cannot be resolved at the current level. The issue exceeds the employee's authority O scope: The situation is urgent; time-sensitive, o has potential business/operational impact

4-Terms Terminology: 4.1- [HIPAA Release Form]: A signed authorization that permits the release of a patient's medical records 4.2- Itemized Bill: detailed invoice that lists each individual service, procedure, medication, O supply provided to patient; along with the corresponding charges foreach item. 4.3- Balance Statement: balance statement is financial summary provided by a healthcare provider, insurance company, or organization that lists all charges, payments, adjustments, and remaining balances for a patient 4.4- SOAP Notes: method of documenting   patient information   using four   categories: Subjective, Objective, Assessment, and Plan: It is a widely used format for medical documentation. Subjective: The patient's reported symptoms, history, and experiences_ Objective: Measurable data such as physical exams, test results, and observations Assessment: The healthcare provider's analysis of the situation, including diagnoses Plan: The treatment Or actions to be taken; including therapy goals and interventions.

6_ Procedures: 6.1- Receive Request: Incoming requests via email, fax, or phone are relayed to the Medical Records Staff: When request is   received_ the staff logs it in the medical records   requests   sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-16),  including   requester name, patient details, and requested documents. This creates verifiable record and initiates the processing workflow_ 6.2- Verify HIPAA Release Form: Upon logging the request, the staff checks   whether valid HIPAA Release Form is attached or   previously filed   If unavailable, the   requester is contacted and asked to provide signed authorization: Processing does not continue until compliant form is received. Once validated, the authorization is archived for future reference_ 6.3- Retrieve Medical Records: With valid authorization on file, the medical   records  staff retrieves the necessary documents   from EMR System_ Examples include SOAP notes, itemized bills, balance statements, Or progress reports. Retrieved documents are reviewed for accuracy before release
6.4- Send Records to Requester: Completed document sets are released through the requester' $ preferred method email o fax. Transmission details (date, method, destination) are logged in the Medical Records Request Sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-l6)_ 7-Revision Amendment Log: Revision # Date Changes 01.01.2026 Document Creation_

6_ Procedures: 6.1- Gathering Information: 6.1.1- Downloading Authorization Report (code: PS/B&A-F-17) since today till the end of the year from WebPT. 6.1.2 - Downloading benefits verification report (code: PS/B&A-F-18) for the next day if we are generating (2nd. 3rd Or 4th report), and generating report for the day after tomorrow if we are generating the 1st report from WebPT 6.1.3- Collecting information about pending cases and pending cases (code: PS/B&A-F- 19) sheet of PTOC Authorization sheet 6.1.4- Collecting   information about   patients on hold   from hold system sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-20): 6.2- Data Preparation 6.2.1- Sort Authorization Report Sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-17) by authorization start date in descending order and filter only active authorizations. 6.2.2- Filter benefits verification report (code: PS/B&A-F-18) choose visit status that is not equal to no-show or cancelled, and appointment type that is not in this list (blocked schedule, consultation, school INDV, group session, El multiple services, El single service, center INDV, teletherapy, multiple DOE, provider out; Madison square, program eval)  6.2.3- From workload sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-21) we filter out all request statuses except appointment [Not Invoiced, On Appeal PZP , Denied, Cancelled Session, Wrong Appointment Type, Duplicate, and Patient Discharged]: 6.2.4- From pending cases sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-19), we filter out pending cases oly from request status 6.3- Data Modification and Transformation 63.1- If we are working on 2nd 3rd or 4th report; we match  patients from this BV generated and prepared report and previous BV report to gather patients added on the system between both reports generations, we filter them out in workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21) to work on them. Patients on hold for pending authorization, while there is an active authorization on the system Patients   on hold for coverage issue and the current insurance is active with no credentialing O policy issues Patients on hold for missing referral while there is referral attached among the documents on patient's chart (code: PS/B&A-F-1l) 6.3.8- Filter out the patients who are not on hold for any reason, with active authorization, and move them to workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21): Moreover; from non-AV and cleared patients, select patients with insurances not requiring authorization and append them to workload sheet  (code:   PSIB&A-F-21): Add   column to check whether the authorization is on the correct case or not 6.3.9- Filter out all patients with non-AV status and move them into workload sheet code: PSIB&A-F-21) 6.3.10- Filter out all patients with initial examination and new cases from non-AV sheet and move them to workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21)

Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms LPurpose: This procedure defines the standardized process for accurately receiving, reviewing; posting; reconciling, and reporting all payments insurance, patient, and third-party ensuring financial accuracy, compliance, and transparency across billing operations_ 2- Scope: This   procedure applies to the   billing team  responsible for  processing payments in the organization. It covers all payment types such as insurance payments (Check; ERA; EFT); patient payments (Cash, Check), third-party payments, payments requiring portal verification 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Insurance Payments: Payments made by an insurance company to the healthcare provider for services rendered to a patient: These may be in the form of checks, Electronic Funds Transfers (EFT), Or wire transfers_ 4.2- Patient Payments: Payments made by patients for their portion of healthcare costs, such as copayments, deductibles, and coinsurance. These can be made using various methods like credit/debit cards, cash; or checks.

4.3- Explanation of Benefits (EOB): A document provided by an insurance company that explains how claim was processed, including the amount covered, patient responsibility (e.g , co-pays), and reasons for denials or adjustments_ 4.4- Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA): An electronic version of the Explanation of Benefits (EOB) that is sent to the healthcare provider; detailing payment processing information, including amounts paid, adjustments, or denials. 4.5- Portal Posting: The process of manually logging into an insurance provider's portal to verify and post payments if an Explanation of Benefits EOB) has not been received or uploaded by the client.
4.6- Patient Account: A record in the billing system that contains all financial information related to specific patient; including services rendered, payments made, and outstanding balances_ 4.7- Eligibility Sheets: tool used by billing teams to track and record payments for patients treated on specific date, ensuring that payments are posted accurately 4.8- Overpayment: situation where the payment received exceeds the amount owed by the patient O insurance company for a service. 4.9- Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT): An electronic method of transferring payments directly from the payer's bank account to the healthcare provider s bank account_ EFT ensures faster; more secure, and automated receipt of payments compared to paper checks

6_ Procedures: 6.1- Receiving Payments: 6.1.1- The payment posting process begins when payments are received from insurance companies, patients, or third-party sources. The billing supervisor oversees the receipt and documentation of all incoming funds. Insurance payments may arrive by check; Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) (code: PSIB&A-F-25), or wire transfer Patient payments can be received through credit o debit cards, cash; Or checks, and must be accurately recorded in patient's payment on the EMR system (e.g-, WebPT) (code: PSIB&A-F-26). 6.1.2- For PT of the City exclusively, any payment_~regardless of source must first be confirmed with the Finance Department before posting: Confirmation is verified through the Transaction Sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-27), which serves as the official record of payments cleared or deposited. No payment for PT of the City is to be posted in the system until Finance verification is completed This control prevents duplication, ensures   accuracy, and maintains   alignment   between Finance and Billing: 6.1.3- Once verified and confirmed, each payment is entered into the billing system and corresponding Tracker sheets (code: PSIB&A-F-24), accessible to the billing  team and department manager. This ensures every payment source is captured and that all cash inflows are accurately recorded for complete financial transparency_ 6.2- Reviewing Payment Documents: 6.2.1- After payments are received, the payment posting specialist reviews all supporting documentation,   including Explanation of Benefits (EOBs) and Electronic Remittance Advices (ERAs) Each document is checked to confirm patient details (name, policy number; etc), payment amount; and any deductions such as co-pays, deductibles, or coinsurance. Adjustments,   including write-offs or contractual reductions, are verified for accuracy. If inconsistencies are found, they- are promptly flagged and reported to the team leader for clarification. This review step prevents posting errors and ensures accurate patient account balances

6.3- Gathering Payments: 6.3.1- Once documentation is verified, the billing team consolidates all payment data to ensure that each transaction matches the appropriate claim. For clients with ERA integration (e.g,, using Therabill" software), payments are automatically imported as ERA files_ For check payments, the EOB accompanying each check is collected and verified_ When clients upload EOBs to the designated Google Drive folder; the Billing Team retrieves them for review. 6.3.2- If an EOB has not been received, the team performs portal posting by logging into the payer portal to confirm payment issuance. Once verified, the payment details are recorded in the eligibility sheets (code: PS/B&A-F-01) corresponding to the date of service. This ensures that all payment data ~electronic, manual, or portal- verified are centralized before posting: 6.4- Entering Payment Information into the System: 6.4.1- After confirming payment details, the billing specialist posts each payment to the correct patient account within the billing system or designated eligibility sheets (code: PS/B&A-F-01) The specialist locates the correct record using the patient' account number; insurance details, Or date of birth: For patients treated at multiple locations, the DOB is used to confirm the correct site. 6.4.2 - The specialist then locates the appropriate service date, allocates the payment, and updates the claim status as follows Paid: Claim paid in full by insurance. Pending: Claim under insurance processing: Deductible: Claim processed but payment is patient'$ responsibility. Denied: Claim rejected; requires denial-management review. 6.4.3- For denials, the reason from the EOB is entered in the *details" column of the eligibility sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-01) Once posted, the updated sheet is visible to the Billing Team, Client Relations, and Department Manager: This step guarantees accurate allocation of funds and enables timely follow-up on denials.

6.5- Handling Payment Discrepancies: 6.5.1- When a payment does not match the expected amount or claim record, the payment posting senior investigates the discrepancy. The process begins with reviewing the EOB, ERA and supporting documents to determine the cause. If clarification is needed, the specialist contacts the payer or patient directly. Based 0n the findings, the account is adjusted to correct   overpayments or underpayments, with  each adjustment documented and justified. Unresolved issues are escalated to the team leader or department manager_ All corrections and communications are recorded in the system and reflected in reconciliation reports, preserving revenue integrity and ensuring a full audit trail_
6.6 Reporting and Documentation: 6.6.1- At the end of each day and month, the Billing Specialist compiles all payment records and prepares required reports. Copies Of EOBs, ERAs, and payment records are retained for reference and audit. Any identified discrepancies are documented with details of their resolution: Patient balance reports are   updated to include adjustments and reconciliations_ 6.6.2-A Daily Payment Report (code: PS/B&A-F-28) summarizing all posted payments is submitted to the department manager: At month-end, monthly report comparing posted payments with financial records is prepared for management review. These reports provide transparency, ensure financial accuracy, and confirm that all billing operations align with company standards_ 7-Revision Amendment Log: Revision # Date Changes 01.01.2026 Document Creation_

1 Purpose: The purpose of this procedure is to establish clear and standardized process for verifying insurance coverage and determining authorization requirements, collecting and submitting prior , authorization requests accurately and 0n time, monitoring and managing pending, denied, or expired authorizations to prevent delays in patient care, and defining clear guidelines for placing patients on hold with appropriate follow-up actions to ensure timely resolution and payment: These processes aim to prevent claim denials, ensure timely treatment authorization; and maintain accurate patient and billing records. 2- Scope: This procedure applies to authorization agents and senior specialists involved in insurance verification; prior authorization, submission and tracking of requests, denial management and escalation, as well as placing patients on hold and ensuring proper follow-up and clearance: 3_References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology:

Authorization (PTOC) Procedure Billing Authorization Department Rev Date 0o.00.00oo 4.1- Authorization Medical  Necessity Medical Review: Approval obtained from an insurance provider confirming coverage of the prescribed treatments 4.2- Initial Evaluation: The first session conducted by physical therapist to assess the patient'$ condition 4.3- Plan of Care (POC): document outlining recommended treatments, frequency, and duration_ 4.4- Patient Filtration: A daily review to ensure patients who attended the clinic have valid authorizations 4.5- EMR System: Electronic system used for scheduling; clinical documentation, and patient records_
4.6- ICD-10 Code: Standardized codes for diagnoses and medical conditions. 4.7-CPT Code: Codes describing medical procedures and services _ 4.8- Modifier: Additional code providing more details about a service. 4.9- Credentialing Information: Provider identification and verification details (NPI, tax ID, location). 4.10- Medical Necessity Denial: Denial from insurance indicating lack of medical necessity. 4.11- Referral Form: Document from referring provider recommending treatment: 4.12- Progress Notes: Clinical documentation recorded by the healthcare provider to track patient' condition; treatment, and progress over time. This includes initial notes and re- evaluations, which are follow-up assessments used to review and update the patient '$ status and response to treatment_ 4.13- Credentialing Issues: Problems related to a provider'$s participation Or network status_ 4.14- Peer-to-Peer Review: Discussion between provider and insurance reviewer to advocate for approval. 4.15- Negative IME: An Independent   Medical Examination (IME) conducted by the government   for Workers Compensation (WC) or No-Fault (NF) patients to assess medical necessity. negative IME indicates that the patient has   reached Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI).

6- Procedures:
6.1- Obtaining Filtering Daily Patient Report: 6.1.1- The authorization   specialist  retrieves   yesterday's patient list from the EMR and documents it in the workload sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-21). 6.1.2- Filters the list to identify patients needing new authorizations 6.1.3- Adds required cases only to workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21). Output: Updated list of required authorization cases_ Communication: Sheet shared with authorization, submission teams, client relations and collection: 6.2- Submission of Daily Authorization Requests: 6.2.1- Specialist prepares authorization requests from the cases entered in the workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21) 6.2.2- Submits via payer portal, fax, or phone, attaching clinical documents and referral if needed. 6.2.3- Records confirmation numbers and dates in the workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21): Output: Completed submissions with confirmation details. Communication: Updates visible in shared sheet with authorization, submission teams, client relations and collection_ 6.3- Pending Authorizations Review: 6.3.1- Every two days; specialist reviews all ""pending" cases. 6.3.2- Follows up with payers for cases exceeding expected timelines. 6.3.2- Updates the workload sheet (code: PS/B&A-F-21). Output: Updated pending authorization statuses. Communication: Escalations sent to senior specialist if needed. 6.4- Denial Management & Investigations: 6.4.1- Upon receiving a denial, specialist reviews the denial reason_ 6.4.2- Coordinates with providers or the auditor to gather missing details. 6.4.3- Escalates complex cases to senior specialist: 6.4.4- Records findings and actions in the workload sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-21): Output: Documented reason and next action. Communication: Updates   shared via   authorization sheet and to the authorization senior_

Output: Updated incomplete notes list and resolved gaps Communication: Auditing team & authorization team notified. 6.6- Hold Categories & Clearance Procedures: This part explains cases when putting the patient on hold is necessary and the applied protocol in each scenario: 6.6.1- Referral Issues: Hold applied when referral is missing, incorrect, or unsigned. Output: Referral hold entry_ Communication: Referral coordinators team receive these data and start working on it: 6.6.2- Pending Authorization Requests: Holds reviewed when insurance pending exceeds 14 days;  or MD does not respond after 14 days: Output: Hold with follow-up date Communication: MD/payer contacted for update_ 6.6.3-Re-Evaluation Rules:
10+ visits approved Proceed normal_ 6-9 visits approved 57 Once a week (except Healthfirst). Healthfirst: 4th request once week; Sth+ request Hold.
<5 visits approved 4 Hold until further approval.
Non-MTG <9 visits L Once a week: Output: Updated frequency or hold applied. Communication: PT and the front desks notified through EMR and the Hold System.

It ensures that all processes are carried out in professional, accurate; and timely manner to maintain compliance,  support   operational efficiency, and enhance overall patient and client satisfaction: 2-Scope: The scope of this procedure includes verifying insurance coverage for patients, handling workers' compensation and no-fault cases, managing inbound and outbound calls for benefits and eligibility verification, maintaining documentation and records for revenue cycle inquiries, managing payments and copays, coordinating with other departments for inquiries received via Google Spaces o calls by forwarding concerns related to billing  authorizations, medical records, or clinical matters with all necessary patient details, and facilitating communication with clients and patients 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015.

4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Client/Patient: Refers to the individual receiving physical therapy services O inquiring about services 4.2- Eligibility: The process of verifying if the patient 's insurance plan is active and covers physical therapy services. 4.3- Benefits Verification: The process of confirming specific insurance benefits, such as copay, deductible, and visit limits. 4.5- Self-Pay: Patients who pay out-of-pocket. Before insurance begins to cover visits O who does not have insurance to cover 4.6-Bundle: pre-paid package of visits for self-pay patients_ 4.7- Copay: A fixed amount paid by the patient at each visit, as required by their insurance 4.8- Deductible The amount a patient must pay out-of-pocket before insurance begins to cover services 4.9- Coinsurance: A percentage of the service cost that the patient pays after the deductible is met 4.10-WC Case (Workers' Compensation): case related to workplace injuries, covered by employer's insurance 4.11- NF Case (No-Fault): case related to auto accidents, covered by no-fault insurance_ 4.12- Client Relations Sheet: The team s internal document used to track eligibility, calls, follow-ups, and case updates_ 4.13-Google Spaces: The primary communication platform used by the team to coordinate tasks and share updates. 4.14- Front Desk Clinic-based staff responsible for scheduling, collecting payments, and patient check-ins.

11- Appendices and Forms L Purpose: The purpose of this document is to provide a clear and detailed procedure for submitting medical claims to insurance companies. It outlines the steps necessary to ensure accurate and timely submission of claims_ 2- Scope: This document is intended for administrative and billing staff, and insurance claim processors who are responsible for submitting medical claims on behalf ofthe practice or facility. It covers the entire process of claim submission, from gathering patient information to submitting claims through the appropriate channels (WebPT- Billing, Claim MD, etc.) and ensuring that all required modifiers, documentation, and authorizations are included. The procedure applies to all types of claims, including commercial insurance, Workers' Compensation (WC), and No- Fault (NF) claims_ 3-References: 3.1- HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) Compliance for secure handling of patient health and billing information. 3.22- Payer-Specific   billing   guidelines,  including Medicare guidelines, Medicaid, and commercial insurance policies:

3.3- CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) Guidelines For claims submission requirements, billing codes, and modifier usage. 3.4- CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) and ICD-10 Coding Manuals For accurate coding of procedures and diagnoses 3.5- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4_Terms_ Terminology: 4.1-Claim: request for payment submitted to an insurance payer for services provided to a patient: 4.2- Primary Insurance: The patient' s main insurance payer that is billed first 4.3- Secondary Insurance: The insurance payer billed after the primary has processed the claim (if applicable) 4.4- Payer: The insurance company or organization responsible for reimbursing medical claims 4.5- Clearinghouse: third-party service that reviews and transmits electronic claims between providers and payers_ 4.6- E-Submission: Electronic submission of claims through the billing software or clearinghouse. 4.7- Timely Filing Limit: The maximum time frame allowed by the payer to accept a claim after the date of service 4.8- EOB (Explanation of Benefits): A statement from the insurance payer explaining how a claim was processed and paid. 4.9- Resubmission: Submitting a corrected claim after a rejection or denial. 4.10- Submission Sheet: The internal document or tracker used to record claim submission details for follow-up and audit purposes_

Review all pended claims daily and ensure the  accuracy and completeness of patient   demographics, insurance, and claim- related information before submission. Perform thorough claim scrubbing to identify and correct any missing,  inaccurate, or invalid data in accordance with internal checklists and compliance Agents (Submission standards. Submit claims manually through WebPT Billing when Billing Specialists) electronic submission is not possible, ensuring   adherence to defined submission timelines and confirming successful submission. Monitor daily rejection reports from the clearinghouse,  identify root causes, correct errors promptly, resubmit claims without delay; and document all actions and updates to support timely and accurate claims processing: Ensures that the provider and location are appropriate for the Credentialing specific insurance plan being submitted. They also handle the Department enrollment of each provider with the relevant insurance companies to ensure eligibility and proper claim submission.

Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms LPurpose: 1.1- The purpose of this procedure is to ensure accurate and timely billing for DOE pediatric services, proper invoicing, and submission for reimbursement_ 1.2- This procedure aims to streamline the process for handling DOE pediatric procedures, ensuring accurate billing and timely payments, and serve as a guideline for the Review process carried out by QA department 2- Scope: This procedure is intended for all personnel involved in the processing of DOE billing for pediatric patients_ 3_References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- DOE: Department of Education The  government  agency responsible for overseeing and ensuring that educational programs and services comply with state and federal regulations In the context of pediatric services, DOE may refer to the department that governs the standards for educational services provided to students with disabilities
Page 2 of 9

DOE Pediatrics Procedure Billing Authorization Department Rev Date 0o.00.00oo 4.2- EMR (Electronic Medical Record): digital version of patients medical chart, containing important health information, appointment details, and therapy documentation, used for scheduling  patient care, and billing purposes. 4.3- Vendor Portal (vPortal): The Department of Education S online management tool for vendors_ The vPortal is used for submitting and managing electronic invoices, tracking payments, and handling all vendor-related activities in the billing - process. It provides a secure, organized platform for vendors to interact with the Department of Education to ensure timely reimbursement 4.4- RSA: Risk Services Agreement: formal document that   outlines the terms and conditions of healthcare   service agreement; including responsibilities, liabilities, and expectations_ 4.5- Bid: proposal for services and costs created by the clinic. A fter the agreement is signed and submitted, the clinic owner must approve the bid before billing can begin. 4.6- Providers: Healthcare professionals (e.g; , therapists, doctors who provide DOE services to children; such as physical therapy, speech therapy, O occupational therapy, and are responsible for documenting patient progress and needs_ 4.7- Service Coordinator: key role in  managing and coordinating   patient-related information. The service coordinator ensures that all necessary agreements and documents, such as the RSA, are received, verified, and submitted for district approval. They also assist with communication between the billing team and other departments to resolve issues. 4.8- Billing Specialist: staff member who reviews, processes, and ensures the accuracy of   billing documentation; including EL service notes, before submitting them for payment: 4.9- Billing Manager: The person in charge of overseeing the billing team, ensuring compliance with billing procedures, and managing the overall billing process for DOE   services within the organization:

4.11-Front Office:
The administrative team is responsible for managing patient appointments, handling patient communications, and ensuring that invoices are prepared for billing submission. 4.12- Front Desk Manager: The person overseeing the front office team; ensuring proper appointment scheduling and smooth operations for patient registration and communication 4.13- Clinic Doctor Owner: The physician Or clinic owner who oversees the operation of the clinic, ensuring that all procedures are following regulations and organizational policies 4.14- Quality Assurance: Quality Assurance (QA) ensures that   services, processes; and documentation meet established standards_
4.15- District Office Representative:
role within the district office that handles the review and approval of agreements and contracts   related to patient care. The District  Office Representative ensures that  all agreements are in place before patient is entered into the system for billing purposes_ They also verify the accuracy of the information before it is sent to the provider_

The patient is then entered into the SESIS portal, visible on the SESIS Patient (code: PSIB&A-F-43).

doctor reviews and accepts the bid in the SESIS portal bid acceptance screen:
6.3.2- After acceptance, the bid is closed and awarded, visible on the SESIS bid awarded clearing the patient for appointment scheduling: 6.4- Appointment Scheduling (Front Office): 6.4.1- Following bid approval, the front office schedules the patient' s appointment and records the details in the WebPT schedule (code: PS/B&A-F-04): 6.4.2- The billing= specialist retrieves the appointment details for billing purposes in the DOE WebPT Sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-44) 6.5- Patient Visit & Paper Invoice (Provider Front Desk): 6.5.1- The provider meets with the patient and documents all services provided. 6.5.2- The provider sends the completed paper invoice (code: PS/B&A-F-45) to the front desk;,  which uploads it to the designated   folder in google drive for the billing specialist: 6.6- Invoice Review & Entry: 6.6.1- The billing specialist reviews the paper invoice in the drive for accuracy_ 6.6.2- Once verified, the billing specialist enters the invoice details into the Vendor Portal vPortal). This   process involves   navigating   the vPortal interface selecting  the appropriate invoice type and associating the services with the correct student profile in vPortal data entry (code: PSIB&A-F-46) before final submission. 6.7- Billing Issues: 6.7.1- After entering the sessions, the billing specialist identifies and reports any issues using the billing issue reporting form (code: PSIB&A-F-47) to the front office O service coordinator_ 6.7.2- The service coordinator or front desk provides the corrected information to the billing specialist for revision.

Service Agreement
The Provider/Payee must obtain security clearance from the DOE; including background and fingerprint checks, for the Provider before service may begin. The Payee must also obtain such clearance for all personnel with access to the student'$ records before such access is granted The Payee must maintain all clearance(s) for the full duration of service provision and/or access t0 student records.
The Provider/Payee must ensure that the Provider' $ certification/license, registration; and security clearance remain current for the full duration of service provision:
The Provider must inform the DOE of the initiation and termination dates of service via the Interactive Voice Response (IVR) system, O other system specified by the DOE 10. The Provider/Payee will keep records necessary t0 disclose the extent and nature of services the Provider furnishes to the student; including contemporaneously submitting Encounter Attendance and Session Notes via the SESIS system or another mechanism specified by the DOE, at no additional cost to the DOE. Such attendance entries and notes must be provided for all sessions and contain all information required by the DOE_ I1. Payee must maintain and produce all records that support service provision and billing for audit Or other review; including  provider/employee/consultant time and payroll records, and must cooperate with all government investigations. Records must be retained for Six years from the date of RSA-2 approval by the DOE, the date the record was created, or the date of transmission or entry into DOE systems, whichever is latest, or longer if specifically required by the DOE_
12. The Payee will ensure that the Provider prepares and submits Student Progress Reports upon request, attend IEP conferences as required, and complete the appropriate pages of the student's IEPs at no cost t0 the DOE 13. By signing this agreement, the Provider/Payee fully accepts all New York State (NYS) and DOE Medicaid requirements including the terms of the NYS Provider Agreement and the NYS Statement of Reassignment: The Provider agrees to remit all Medicaid reimbursements to the DOE, and not bill Medicaid directly for any services that the DOE will bill for under the school health program. Upon request; the Provider/Payee agrees to furnish the NYS Department of Health or its designee, the US Department of Health and Human Services, and the NYS Medicaid Fraud Control   Unit with   requested information on the services   provided to the   Student: The Provider/Payee agrees to comply with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Section 504 of the Federal Rehabilitation Act; and all other State and Federal statutory and constitutional non-discrimination provisions, and to abide by all applicable Federal and State laws and regulations, including the Social Security Act; the New York Social Services Law, Part 42 of the CER, and Title 18 of the NYCRR The Provider/Payee must be familiar with and adhere to the DOE's Medicaid Compliance Plan ~the Plan"). The Provider/Payee must immediately notify the DOE if the Provider is excluded from any Federal health care program or NYS Medicaid program
14. The Provider must have a National Provider Identifier (NPI) number; which can be obtained at the NPPES website. That number must be entered in the appropriate field of each electronic invoice: 15. Payment shall be made at the applicable RSA rate established by the DOE for the discipline, district/borough, and language. Such rate shall be paid solely for time spent providing direct services and shall not vary with the size of the group served, except where specifically and explicitly indicated on the RSA schedule. If Payee is also under separate contract with the DOE to provide the same discipline, district/borough and service, payment for services under this Agreement will be paid in accordance with the rates stated in such separate contract Invoices pursuant to this Agreement must be submitted electronically on a monthly basis through a system designated by

Provider and Payee are prohibited from giving gifts to DOE staff, parents, Or students regardless of value.
18. Pursuant to the New York City Conflict of Interest Board (NYC COIB) Rules, a current full-time employee of the DOE cannot provide services under this Agreement unless the services are covered by the mass waivers granted by the NYC COIB, Or an individual waiver is granted by the NYC COIB based on a request from the DOE Part-time DOE employees need individual  waivers. Individuals who leave the employ of the DOE including those performing per diem Or part-time work_ may not provide services under this Agreement for one full year after leaving DOE employment unless they obtain an individual waiver from the NYC COIB_
19. If the Payee'$ aggregate business with the DOE during any twelve-month period is projected by the DOE to exceed S100,000,it must complete VENDEX questionnaires, submit them to the NYC Mayor'$ Office of Contract Services, and enter into a standard contract with the DOE_ The Payee agrees that it is not entitled t0 payment during any twelve-month period in excess of S1OO,000 without entering into a contract with the DOE_ 20. Without prior approval from the DOE Representative, Provider o Payee may not provide Related Services to the Student if the Provider/Payee had previously performed a related services evaluation O assessment of the Student: 21. The Provider/Payee must carry professional malpracticelliability insurance for the services provided under this Agreement_
22. If the Provider/Payee fails to adhere fully to any of the above-stated conditions 7 through 21, the DOE reserves the right to withhold payment for Services The DOE reserves the right to withhold payment if there is any dispute with the Provider; the Payee, or any entity affiliated with the Provider and/or Payee. 23. The DOE may terminate this Agreement upon 10 days' prior written notice to the Parent/Guardian and the Provider/Payee, Or immediately for cause_

Terms Terminology 5- Responsibility for Use and Preservation 6- Procedures 7- Revision Amendment Log 8- Document Distribution List 9- Procedure Execution Time (SLA) 10- Process Performance Indicator Measurement 11 - Appendices and Forms LPurpose: This procedure defines the process for billing and authorization for early intervention pediatric care.
2-Scope: This procedure governs all personnel involved in the processing of early intervention (EI) notes for pediatric patients 3-References: 3.1- The International Standard ISO 9001.2015. 4- Terms Terminology: 4.1- Early intervention: service provided to children with developmental delays o health problems, including therapies such as physical therapy, speech therapy, and occupational therapy, designed to address issues early in a child'$ development: 4.2- EMR (Electronic Medical Record): digital version of a patient's medical chart; containing important health information, appointment details, and therapy documentation, used for scheduling, patient care, and billing purposes.

4.3-Challenge Portal: An online  platform used for submitting  completed Early Intervention notes; billing documents, and related materials for reimbursement processing: 4.44- Billing Sheets (Challenge Sheets): tool used for billing each Early Intervention session, containing patient El IDs and session details_ The sheet helps calculate the amount due per session and tracks patient visits for accurate billing: 4.5- Cancellation Notes: Notes that  document   any patient appointment cancellations, including the reason for cancellation and relevant dates, submitted as part of the billing documentation for correct processing and reimbursement_ 4.6- Providers: Healthcare professionals (e.g , therapists, doctors who provide early intervention services to children, such as physical therapy, speech therapy, Or occupational therapy, and are responsible for documenting patient progress and needs 4.7- Service Coordinator: The person responsible for managing and coordinating the care for the child, combining the therapy notes with signature logs, and submitting the documentation to the Billing Specialist for review 4.8- Billing Specialist: staff member who reviews, processes, and ensures the accuracy of   billing documentation, including EI service notes, before submitting them for payment: 4.9- Billing Manager: The person in charge of overseeing the billing team, ensuring compliance with billing procedures, and managing the overall billing   process for EI services within the organization 4.10- Finance Department: The department is responsible for managing the financial aspects of the organization, including   tracking  payments,  addressing billing   discrepancies, and following up on outstanding payments for EI services.

4.11- Front Desk:
The administrative team responsible for   scheduling  appointments, managing patient records in the EMR system; and facilitating   communication between   patients and healthcare providers_ 4.12- Front Desk Manager: The person overseeing the front desk team; ensuring proper appointment scheduling and smooth operations for patient registration and communication: 4.13- Clinic Doctor Owner: The physician O clinic owner who oversees the operation of the clinic, ensuring that all procedures are compliant with regulations and organizational policies 5-Responsibility for Use and Preservation: Title Responsibility Responsible for ensuring the processes described in this procedure achieve their intended outcomes_ CEO Responsible for ensuring that the procedure requirements are integrated within business processes_ Responsible for accurately   completing EI session  notes, resolving any Providers identified issues or required corrections, and uploading finalized documentation to the designated system for processing: Responsible for scheduling   patient   appointments in the EMR system including any cancellations Consultant in ensuring that patient information is correctly entered into the Front Desk system and updated as needed: Accountable for assisting in patient communication, helping to coordinate appointment details. Responsible for reviewing submitted EL notes for accuracy and alignment with EMR appointments, coordinating corrections with providers and the service Billing Specialist coordinator, completing cancellation and billing documentation, submitting records to the challenge portal, and following up to ensure timely payment processing-

Title
Responsibility Responsible for overseeing the billing ~ team to ensure compliance with billing procedures and accurate processing of EL services Accountable for   ensuring the billing   process is efficient and   effective, Billing Manager addressing any issues or discrepancies that arise. Accountable for   reviewing   billing   documentation and make necessary adjustments or approvals as needed Responsible for handling the financial aspects of the organization, including payment tracking and reconciliation. Finance Consultant in addressing any billing discrepancies, assist in resolving payment Department issues, and ensure proper reimbursement for El services_ Accountable for following up on outstanding payments Accountable for managing the front office team; ensuring smooth appointment scheduling and patient check-in; Front Desk Accountable for   overseeing patient communication and coordination of Manager necessary information between the office and healthcare providers_ Accountable for addressing patient concerns and ensure proper procedures are followed: 6- Procedures: 6.1- Authorization Receipt: 6.1.1- The service coordinator receives patient authorization from the early intervention center (Challenge), referred to as the challenge authorization form (code: PS/B&A- F-50): 6.1.2- The authorization includes the approved specialty O specialties and the mandate, specifying the number of sessions per week and session duration (details are found on the challenge authorization form (code: PS/B&A-F-50) 6.1.3- Upon receiving the authorization, the service coordinator informs the front desk to begin scheduling appointments_ 6.2- Appointment Scheduling: 6.2.1- The front desk schedules the patient's appointment; recording it in the WebPT Clinic Schedule (code: PSIB&A-F-04): 6.2.2- The billing specialist extracts relevant appointment details for billing purposes_

6.3- Patient Visit & Paper Notes Filling (Provider): 6.3.1- The provider conducts the therapy session with the patient: 6.3.2- The provider completes the corresponding paper session note form code: PS/B&A- F-5l) documenting the services provided during the session: 6.3.3- The provider submits the completed notes to the service coordinator. 6.4- Combining Documentation (Service Coordinator): 6.4.1- The service coordinator combines the paper session note form (code: PS/B&A-F-51) and the signature log form (code: PS/B&A-F-52), recording dates of attendance and CPT codes for each session_ 6.4.2- The combined documentation is forwarded to the billing specialist: 6.5- Reviewing Notes Billing (Billing Specialist): 6.5.1- Once the notes are verified, the billing specialist begins the billing process_ 6.5.2- The billing specialist reviews the notes and cross-checks them with the sheet (code: PSIB&A-F-45) for discrepancies Or missing information. 6.6- Reporting Issues (Billing Specialist): 6.6.1- If any issues are discovered, the billing specialist informs the provider using the issue reporting form (code: PS/B&A-F-47). 6.7- Correcting Issues: 6.7.1- The provider addresses the issues raised by the billing specialist, makes the necessary corrections, and returns the updated notes via the service coordinator. 6.8- Documentation Completion: 6.8.1- The billing specialist finalizes the documentation after corrections are made. 6.8.2- This includes verifying all notes, finalizing cancellation note forms (PS/B&A-F-53), and completing the challenge billing sheet (PSIB&A-F-54). 6.9- Submission to Challenge Portal: 6.9.1- The billing specialist compiles all required documentation (including notes, challenge billing sheet (PSIB&A-F-54), and cancellation note   forms (PS/B&A-F-53), and submits them through the "Upload" tab o the challenge portal (code: PSB&A-F- 55) for processing and reimbursement:

Appendices and Forms L Purpose: This procedure establishes a structured process to ensure that all projects are effectively monitored, tracked, and aligned with organizational objectives, ISO requirements, and client expectations. It defines project   follow-up, communication   (including Voice   Ticketing System) , risk and change management, and reporting: 2-Scope: 2.1- Applies to all departments participating in project execution, monitoring; and reporting, including PMO, Operations, QA, Finance, IT, and other functional teams 2.2 - Covers the entire project lifecycle, from initiation to closure, for internal projects, client projects, and externally funded initiatives. 3-References
3.1- ISO 9001.2015
3.2- Communication Plan (PSITM-F-08)
3.3- Document Control Procedure (PSIQA-P-01)

4- Terms Terminology 4.1- Project Manager (PM): Individual responsible for project execution;, milestones, and deliverables_ 4.2 - Project Management Team (PMT): Cross-functional oversight team for monitoring and coordination_ 4.3- Project Follow-Up Form (PS/PM-F-01): Document for tracking milestones, deliverables, responsibilities, and status. 4.4- Project Review Minutes (PS/PM-F-02): Template for recording meetings, decisions, and action items_ 4.5- Risk and Issue Register (PS/PM-F-03): Document for tracking project-specific risks and issues_ 4.6- Voice Ticketing System (VTS): Platform for logging voice updates,  feedback,  and escalations_
4.7- Project Charter: Defines scope, objectives, milestones, resources, and risks

6- Procedures:
6k-Project Suggestion Prioritization and _Launch: Projects proposed by Department Heads or Head of OPEX: Prioritization via Project Priority Matrix by PM Head and Top Management PM assigned by PM Head; forms project team with departmental reps. Project Charter prepared and approved by CEO. 62_Preject Follow-Up: PM updates the steps of project weekly PS/PM-F-01. Updates communicated via VTS to PM Head, QA, Project Team; and Top Management (for high-priority projects). It recorded in support request form code (PS/OpEx-F-01) Records are retained 3 years. 6.3-Progress Review Meetings: 6.3.1 Meeting Structure: PMT: Head of OPEX, PM Head, Project Manager, QA, Functional Leads, Top Management for strategic projects. Frequency by priority: Priority Frequency High Weekly Medium Biweekly Low Monthly PM schedules meetings attendees determined from Project Charter and Communication Plan_ 6.3.2 Meeting Minutes: Recorded by Project Coordinator using PS/PM-F-01. Distributed within 48 hours via VTS and email. Action items logged in VTS, tracked to closure. 6.4Interaland Exterual Reporting: 6.4.1 Internal: PM follow up with the Department   Managers and   reported any   delays or communication issues to QA per Communication Plan
